item.title: How to Protect Your Braces During Sports Activities
item.description: Proper mouth protection is recommended by Lasley Orthodontics when you participate in any sports activities. If you wear braces, protecting braces becomes essential. Injuries to your mouth can not only damage your teeth, but your braces could break and cut open your lip. Full Facial Guards Full facial guards are often used in football and […]
item.content:encoded:
Proper mouth protection is recommended by Lasley Orthodontics when you participate in any sports activities. If you wear braces, protecting braces becomes essential. Injuries to your mouth can not only damage your teeth, but your braces could break and cut open your lip.

Full Facial Guards

Full facial guards are often used in football and offer protection to your mouth from most injuries. Even with full facial protection, you may benefit from additional mouth protection. While your face is protected from outside impact, you could still suffer from cuts or damage to your braces from internal impact.

Mouthguards

Mouthguards referred to as boil-and-bites can be purchased at many retail stores. As the name implies, these guards are boiled in water to heat and soften the material. While the guard is still warm, you place it in your mouth and bite down gently. This causes the guard to form to the shape of your mouth. Unfortunately, these guards do not necessarily offer the best protection or fit.

Dr. Andrew Lasley can make custom mouthguards specifically for you. The custom fitting ensures you of better protection and a comfortable fit. Custom guards are also built in layers for durability. The American Dental Association recommends custom guards for orthodontic patients. Your mouthguard will be designed to provide proper protection for both your teeth and your braces.

No matter what type of sport you participate in, a mouthguard can protect your braces. Even an activity as seemingly harmless as table tennis can result in a contact injury. The Academy for Sports Dentistry states that a properly fitted mouthguard should not interfere with any athletic activity.

Lasley Orthodontics will provide you with properly-fitting mouth protection to ensure the safety of your braces and your teeth. We will be glad to answer any questions you have so you can continue the activities you enjoy with little concern. If you do suffer any injuries to your mouth or braces during sporting activities, please contact us immediately. The sooner we can care for your mouth, the better the results will be.

item.title: The Transformation of Valentine’s Day
item.description: Did you know the actions leading to the beginnings of Valentine’s Day were actually centered on the avoidance of war? A Catholic priest named Valentine defied the orders of the Emperor Claudius II and secretly married young men and their brides after the emperor had declared it illegal because only single, young men could be […]
item.content:encoded:
Did you know the actions leading to the beginnings of Valentine’s Day were actually centered on the avoidance of war? A Catholic priest named Valentine defied the orders of the Emperor Claudius II and secretly married young men and their brides after the emperor had declared it illegal because only single, young men could be sent to war. Rather than lose potential soldiers to fight his war, Claudius attempted to hoard them by proclaiming marriage illegal.

Valentine continued to marry young couples anyway and, eventually, was put to death for it in 270 AD. Before his death, he sent a letter to a secret love and signed it “From your Valentine”. Nearly 1,800 years later, people are still signing letters and cards in this manner. This year, carry on the tradition started long ago, while adding your own twist. Here are a few suggestions.

Simple and Creative Valentine’s Day Ideas

  • Memorialize it with a Photo. Couples often have photos taken around Christmas, but Valentine’s Day photos allow you to capitalize on romance. Famous couple Julia Child and her husband, Paul, had their picture taken together every Valentine’s Day and included their sense of humor with silly props.
  • Return to Your First Date Location. Even if your first date together was at a local hotdog stand, its sentimental value can make it a fun part of your Valentine’s Day agenda. Be creative and make a treasure hunt with clues that lead your partner to the original date location, where you can express your love with flowers or a gift.
  • “From Your Valentine” Messages. Deliver your message in a creative way to make this Valentine’s Day stand out from the others. Bake your partner’s favorite treat and write a message on it with a tube of icing, or draw a note on the steamed up mirror so it shows up when your partner takes a shower.

Although Valentine’s Day is a day to celebrate love, it doesn’t have to be a special day only for couples. If you’re single, use this special day to shower yourself with love, because you’re worth it! After all, the priest Valentine believed so strongly in the sanctity of love that he was willing to risk his life for it. Whether you’re in a relationship or single, young or old, romantic or not, Valentine’s Day is for you. Happy Valentine’s Day from the orthodontics office of Dr. Andrew Lasley.

item.title: Does My Child Need Two-phase Orthodontic Treatment?
item.description: Two-phase orthodontic treatment involves two separate and distinct periods that your child receives orthodontic treatment. It allows your son or daughter to begin early treatment of bite and jaw problems, in order to reduce the dental issues he or she experiences later on. Two-phase treatment with Dr. Andrew Lasley can improve how well the second […]
item.content:encoded:
Two-phase orthodontic treatment involves two separate and distinct periods that your child receives orthodontic treatment. It allows your son or daughter to begin early treatment of bite and jaw problems, in order to reduce the dental issues he or she experiences later on.

Two-phase treatment with Dr. Andrew Lasley can improve how well the second phase of the treatment works and helps to make room for permanent teeth. Overall, two-phase treatment helps to position the teeth and the jaw for an attractive profile. Our team at Lasley Orthodontics recommends that you bring your child to our Renton, WA office at the age of seven or eight, so that Dr. Andrew Lasley can determine if early (Phase-One) treatment is necessary.

Phase-One

Phase-One orthodontic treatment is known as early treatment. It begins shortly after your child’s first orthodontic examination, usually around age eight or nine. The main goal of Phase-One orthodontic treatment is to help make room for permanent teeth, which reduces crooked teeth as a result of overcrowding. It treats the jaw and bite growth, and issues like crossbite or underbite. This can reduce the need for your child to undergo extractions.

Phase-Two

Phase-Two orthodontic treatment is when braces are placed on the upper and/or lower teeth. The purpose is not just to correct spaces or misaligned teeth, but also to correct overbite or underbite concerns. Phase-Two usually begins around age 11 or 12, and the braces are worn for an average of two to three years, depending on your child’s unique needs. Some children have fewer issues and wear braces for little more than a year, while others need them for up to four years.

Signs your child needs two-phase orthodontic treatment

If your child exhibits the following signs, he or she may be a good candidate for two-phase orthodontic treatment:

  • Losing baby teeth early, before five years of age
  • Problems with biting or chewing
  • Sucking the thumb after age five
  • Evidence of a crossbite, where the teeth don’t come together when opening or closing of the mouth
  • Teeth are crowded at age seven or eight
  • Protruding teeth on the top or bottom

Not all children need to have early treatment, but if your child shows any of these signs, you should bring him or her to us for an evaluation at Lasley Orthodontics.

item.title: St. Patrick’s Day: Celtic Pride, Green Shamrocks, and Lucky Charms!
item.description: “St. Patrick’s Day is an enchanted time — a day to begin transforming winter’s dreams into summer’s magic.” Adrienne Cook Lucky green shamrocks, leprechauns, and pots of gold – it must be St. Patrick’s Day! If you’re not Irish, how do you go about celebrating St. Patrick’s Day? It’s easy: You just put on one […]
item.content:encoded:
“St. Patrick’s Day is an enchanted time — a day to begin transforming winter’s dreams into summer’s magic.” Adrienne Cook

Lucky green shamrocks, leprechauns, and pots of gold – it must be St. Patrick’s Day! If you’re not Irish, how do you go about celebrating St. Patrick’s Day? It’s easy: You just put on one of those tall leprechauns hats, dress in green from head to toe, and wear one of those carefree pins that say “Kiss Me, I’m Irish”. On St. Patrick’s Day, everyone is Irish, and that is the universal beauty of the holiday. Celtic pride does not discriminate.

Wondering what our team at Lasley Orthodontics is doing to celebrate March 17th? Well, we’ve thought about doing everything from handing out lucky gold coins (you know, the fake ones that are made of chocolate) to shamrock stickers. Maybe we’ll even give away green toothbrushes and floss! You’ll never know unless you come in to see Dr. Andrew Lasley !

All kidding aside, St. Patrick’s Day is an important cultural and religious holiday. There are lavish parades and church services across Ireland on March 17th. Over time, however, the holiday has developed into a day to observe Irish culture in general. In places like England and the United States, where there is a large Irish Diaspora, the holiday has greater significance than other countries. From the streets of Boston to St. Patrick’s Cathedral in New York, it is a day of celebration, and many Americans of Irish descent will cook up a traditional meal of corned beef and cabbage.

So, to all of you with Irish ancestry, and to all of you who have decided to be Irish for the day, our office wishes you a Happy St. Patrick’s Day. Good luck looking for a pot of leprechaun gold, which is said to exist at the end of the rainbow. However, keep away from those sugary Lucky Charms; sweet cereals might taste good, but your kids’ teeth might not be feeling too lucky if they eat it for breakfast every day. Have a great St. Paddy’s Day!

item.title: Five Braces-Friendly Dinner Recipes
item.description: Did you know that more than four million children throughout the US and Canada have braces? At Lasley Orthodontics, Dr. Andrew Lasley and our staff know that kids can be picky and meticulous eaters. If cooking for children without braces is difficult, preparing meals for children with braces is especially daunting “Comfort food takes on a whole […]
item.content:encoded:
Did you know that more than four million children throughout the US and Canada have braces? At Lasley Orthodontics, Dr. Andrew Lasley and our staff know that kids can be picky and meticulous eaters. If cooking for children without braces is difficult, preparing meals for children with braces is especially daunting

“Comfort food takes on a whole new meaning when cooking for children with braces,” says Pamela Waterman, author of The Braces Cookbook: Recipes You and Your Orthodontist Will Love. “Whether you have new brackets, elastics, headgear, or more, there are great foods you can eat; it just takes some thought.”

These five braces-friendly dinner recipes will be sure to keep your kids smiling!

  1. Macaroni and cheese is the ultimate comfort food. Pasta is soft, so it’s easy for children with braces to eat. The warm, gooey cheese melts in the mouth and doesn’t get stuck in the braces like hard or sticky foods. Chances are good that even the pickiest eater has a soft spot for this homespun classic.
  2. The key to braces-friendly cooking is to replace hard, crunchy foods with softer substitutes. In other words, burritos are a better option than tacos, and lasagna is a better choice than pizza. At the same time, if you have the culinary skills to whip up a pizza with a soft crust, you’re going to win the Best Mom (or Dad) of the Year award.
  3. Your child may not like fruits and vegetables. In fact, he or she may even try to convince you that with new braces, fruits and vegetables are off limits. Nice try, kids. While your child is wearing braces, prepare meals with cooked vegetables instead of raw vegetables. A vegetable stir-fry is a healthy and soft dinner choice for kids with braces.
  4. Whether it’s beef or chicken, meat is a good source of protein. However, meat, even when it’s carefully taken off the bone (kids with braces should never eat meat from a bone), can easily get caught in braces. Sloppy Joes are a good alternative. The beef is softened by the addition of the sauce and less likely to get strung in the wires and brackets of the braces. Serve the Sloppy Joes with a side of mashed potatoes.
  5. Ask any child and he or she will tell you that the best part of dinner is dessert. While hard candy, licorice, taffy, caramel, popcorn, and all other chewy candies should be avoided, ice cream and cake are braces-friendly treats that keep kids smiling.

Need more braces-friendly food ideas? Feel free to ask any member of our team.

item.title: Play it Safe This Spring With a Mouthguard
item.description: It’s springtime and it’s again time to remind our patients at Lasley Orthodontics to protect their faces and pearly whites while out on the field with a mouthguard. According to the National Youth Sports Safety Foundation, children, high-school athletes and adults have more than 5,000,000 teeth knocked out in sporting events annually. If you are […]
item.content:encoded:
It’s springtime and it’s again time to remind our patients at Lasley Orthodontics to protect their faces and pearly whites while out on the field with a mouthguard. According to the National Youth Sports Safety Foundation, children, high-school athletes and adults have more than 5,000,000 teeth knocked out in sporting events annually.

If you are planning on participating in spring sports, it’s imperative to have a proper-fitting mouthguard. A mouthguard can prevent chipped or broken teeth, lip and cheek injuries, jaw fractures, mouth lacerations and even concussions.

Having a mouthguard can make the difference between losing your teeth or not, and because many of our patients who play high school sports have jaws that are still growing, last year’s mouthguard may no longer fit as it should. Dr. Andrew Lasley and our team at Lasley Orthodontics can fit you for a new guard.

To learn more about mouthguards or for general questions about your treatment at our Renton, WA office, please give us a call!

item.title: What is Expected of Me During My Orthodontic Treatment?
item.description: We hear this question a lot at Lasley Orthodontics, and we don’t mind when patients who are eager to complete their orthodontic treatment ask us. After all, we know there is no better feeling than getting your braces off! During your initial consultation with Dr. Andrew Lasley, we will map out a specific treatment for […]
item.content:encoded:
We hear this question a lot at Lasley Orthodontics, and we don’t mind when patients who are eager to complete their orthodontic treatment ask us. After all, we know there is no better feeling than getting your braces off!

During your initial consultation with Dr. Andrew Lasley, we will map out a specific treatment for you and will try our hardest to give you a timeline of when we expect you to wrap up treatment.

Having said that, we know every patient is different. People have different biological responses to orthodontic treatment and some people’s teeth may move faster than others.

Luckily, there are things you can do to ensure your treatment wraps up in a timely manner. By following these suggestions, you can avoid any setbacks during your treatment.

  • Make sure to keep your adjustment appointments. Postponing or cancelling will delay treatment!
  • Be sure to show up on time to your appointments. This will give Dr. Andrew Lasley and our team time to do everything we planned during your adjustment visit.
  • When prescribed, make sure you are diligent about wearing rubber bands or other appliances.
  • Avoid damage to your braces and teeth by wearing a mouth guard during sports activities.
  • Make sure to brush and floss regularly! Gum disease or other dental work can delay orthodontic treatment.

If you remember to follow these guidelines, you’ll be on your way to having the dazzling, healthy smile you’ve always wanted. As for us, our team at Lasley Orthodontics will do our part to move your orthodontic treatment along efficiently. If you have any questions about your treatment time, please give us a call at our Renton, WA office or ask us during your next visit!

item.title: Keep That Candy at Bay During Your Treatment
item.description: Now that you have braces, it is more important than ever to maintain good oral hygiene during your treatment, as Dr. Andrew Lasley will tell you. While we trust you will continue brushing and flossing on a regular basis throughout your treatment at Lasley Orthodontics, you also have to mindful of what you eat. While […]
item.content:encoded:
Now that you have braces, it is more important than ever to maintain good oral hygiene during your treatment, as Dr. Andrew Lasley will tell you. While we trust you will continue brushing and flossing on a regular basis throughout your treatment at Lasley Orthodontics, you also have to mindful of what you eat. While all that sweet, sour, and sticky candy may taste great, these treats can actually damage your teeth and braces!

Sour candies can be acidic to your teeth, and actually wear down the enamel that protects them, resulting in tooth decay and cavities. If you do indulge in eating these candies at some point during your treatment, be sure to wash your mouth out with water, drink milk or eat a few slices of cheese. These foods will help neutralize the acid in your mouth.

Besides cavities and tooth decay, many people do not realize that good oral health and hygiene are important to your overall health, too; researchers have discovered the connection between periodontal disease and other major health concerns, such as heart disease. Therefore, it is important to maintain a good oral hygiene routine just as you did before your braces. This includes visiting your dentist here in Renton, WA every six months, brushing and flossing daily, and using an antibacterial mouthwash, as well as visiting Dr. Andrew Lasley for your regular adjustment appointments.

For more questions about foods you should avoid while wearing braces, or if you have any general questions about your treatment, we encourage you to give us a call, ask us at your next adjustment appointment or ask us on Facebook!

item.title: Every Day is Earth Day
item.description: During the early days of the environmental awareness movement, those who demonstrated against pollution, toxic chemicals, and the general public health were known as hippies. The early 1970s were a time of change, and assertions that we needed to pay more attention to the Earth’s atmosphere were generally dismissed. But within a couple decades, it […]
item.content:encoded:
During the early days of the environmental awareness movement, those who demonstrated against pollution, toxic chemicals, and the general public health were known as hippies. The early 1970s were a time of change, and assertions that we needed to pay more attention to the Earth’s atmosphere were generally dismissed. But within a couple decades, it had become clear that the previous generation was right; the citizens of the world needed to become more environmentally conscious.

Many people feel that they can’t make a difference if they don’t do something big. But caring for the environment doesn’t have to be an all-or-nothing concept. In fact, the little things you do can add up to make a great impact, especially in our community. Here are a few ways you can help the environment on Earth Day, April 22nd and all year around.

Four Small Ways to be Environmentally Friendly

  • Recycle Your Textiles. Nearly 21 million tons of textiles are added to American landfills each year, according to the Environmental Protection Agency. Donating your unwanted clothing to a secondhand store or an organization that repurposes fabric helps cut down on solid waste and conserves natural resources.
  • Reduce Usage of Disposables. Plastic bottles and bags, disposable diapers and other things we can use and toss out are convenient, but they’re not necessary. Simply choosing to replace one of type of disposable with a reusable product can help you cut down on waste that has a large negative impact on our environment.
  • Conserve Water. If everyone in the United States turned off the water while brushing their teeth, more than 1.5 million gallons of water could be conserved. Turn the water on long enough to wet your toothbrush for brushing and rinsing, and then immediately turn the water off again.
  • Turn Off the Lights. Flip the light switch to “Off” if you’re going to leave a particular room for 15 minutes or more. This will conserve energy on incandescent light bulbs and cut down on cooling costs.

It’s not necessary to be an activist or install solar panels all over your home to help the environment. Although you can do these things, the little everyday measures make a big difference in helping to conserve energy and the environment, while reducing your carbon footprint. Our team at Lasley Orthodontics wants to remind you to celebrate Earth Day and help the environment, knowing that it will benefit your and your children’s generation.

item.title: The Importance of Wearing Your Retainer After Orthodontic Treatment
item.description: It’s the big day and your braces are finally coming off! Does that mean you are completely done? Not so fast! After you complete your treatment here at Lasley Orthodontics, Dr. Andrew Lasley and our team will recommend you wear a retainer, which must be worn routinely after treatment in order to hold your teeth […]
item.content:encoded:
It’s the big day and your braces are finally coming off! Does that mean you are completely done? Not so fast! After you complete your treatment here at Lasley Orthodontics, Dr. Andrew Lasley and our team will recommend you wear a retainer, which must be worn routinely after treatment in order to hold your teeth in their proper, new position while your gums, ligaments and bones adapt. Most patients are required to wear their retainer every night at first, with many also being directed to wear them during the day. It’s important to know there are different kinds of retainers, and today we thought we would explain the differences between them.

Hawley Retainers

The Hawley retainer is one of the most common types of retainers. It is a removable retainer made of a combination of a metal wire that typically surrounds the six anterior teeth and is designed to keep your teeth in place. This retainer is made from impressions of your teeth so that it fits snugly and comfortably in the roof of your mouth, while the wire and acrylic framing keeps your teeth in an ideal position. The acrylic can also be personalized with a large number of colors or patterns.

Essix (Clear) Retainers

The Essix retainer is a transparent removable retainer that fits over the entire arch of your teeth. This clear or transparent retainer fits over the entire arch of teeth and is produced from a mold. Similar to Invisalign’s clear aligner trays, Essix retainers have no metal or wires. They can also be used to produce minor tooth movements and can be helpful in prevention of tooth wear due to tooth grinding at night.

Bonded Retainers

Bonded lingual retainers are cemented directly to the inside surface of your lower canines. Dr. Andrew Lasley and our team at Lasley Orthodontics encourage our patients with bonded lingual retainers to be careful with their bite as the bonding material may break due to incorrect biting and cause your teeth to shift. As with removable retainers, it is important to keep your bonded retainers clean. When brushing, make sure to carefully clean the inside of your lower teeth, as well as the wire itself.

The retention phase of treatment begins when the patient’s braces are removed. Retainers are worn full time, typically for the first nine months, except while eating. Retainers should also be removed before brushing your teeth.

If you have any questions about the retainers we offer or to learn more about post-orthodontic treatment, please feel free to contact us at our convenient Renton, WA office and we will be happy to answer any of your questions!

An orthodontist in Renton WA such as Dr. Lasley is a leading Invisalign provider for teens as well as adults.

How Much Does Invisalign Cost?

The cost of Invisalign and Invisalign Teen varies for each individual patient. At Lasley Orthodontics, we understand that no two patients have the same exact needs, therefore no two treatment plans are ever identical.

What Determines Your Invisalign Cost?

The Invisalign cost is determined by specific personal factors such as the severity of issues to be corrected, the number of aligners required, and the location of treatment. These factors all help calculate your own Invisalign cost in Renton WA.

How Can I Make The Invisalign Cost More Affordable?

At Lasley Orthodontics, we offer various payment methods and financing options to accommodate any budget. We accept cash, check, debit, as well as most major credit card providers. If your orthodontic care is covered by your insurance, you may receive the benefits of reduced personal expenses.

Does My Insurance Cover Invisalign?

If your insurance provider covers your orthodontic treatment, you can receive the benefits of reduced out of pocket expenses. At Lasley Orthodontics, we keep our patients in mind and accept most major insurance providers.

Finding The Best Invisalign Cost In Renton WA

As a leading orthodontist in Renton, Dr. Lasley provides the highest quality orthodontic care at an affordable price that can accommodate any patient, no matter their budget. How Much Does Invisalign Cost Near Me? Our […]
item.content:encoded:
Where Can I Find Invisalign Near Me?

At Lasley Orthodontics, we specialize in Invisalign and Invisalign Teen in Renton WA. As a leading orthodontist in Renton, Dr. Lasley provides the highest quality orthodontic care at an affordable price that can accommodate any patient, no matter their budget.

How Much Does Invisalign Cost Near Me?

Our team of professionals understand that no two patients have the same needs. Therefore, no two treatment plans are ever exactly alike. The Invisalign cost is based on personal factors such as the location of treatment, length of treatment, and the severity of issues being treated. However, the cost of Invisalign in Renton WA may be cheaper compared to a bigger city or a higher income neighborhood.

Is Invisalign Only For Adults?

We are experts in Invisalign for adults as well as Invisalign Teen, which is made specifically with a teenager’s needs in mind. Our staff understands that appearances can everything to a teen. However, this can be an ideal time for orthodontic treatment. Traditional braces can hinder your child’s appearance and affect their daily life for the entire duration of treatment. As a result, we offer clear aligners that have the power to eliminate all of the negative aspects of braces. Important occasions such as senior prom or yearbook pictures are no longer have to be overshadowed by a mouth full of metal braces. Invisalign Teen can also be removed for extracurricular activities such as sports or band practice.

Why Choose Invisalign Over Braces?

Invisalign offers benefits that are not possible with traditional braces or any other option of orthodontic treatment. Braces have brackets that may hinder your appearance and limit your diet throughout the entire duration of treatment. Traditional braces are composed of archwires and elastics, which can cause discomfort and irritation. Invisalign offers distinct advantages including being:

Removable Enjoy all of your favorite foods, drinks, and activities.
Clear Virtually invisible, providing the positive effects of braces without anyone having to know.
Comfortable Custom-made using smooth plastic to guarantee maximum comfort and perfect fit.

Finding Invisalign Near Me In Renton WA

Dr. Lasley and our team at Lasley Orthodontics are experts in orthodontic treatment. We are here to answer any questions you may have involving Invisalign or Invisalign Teen in Renton WA, Burien WA, West Seattle WA, Kent WA, Maple Valley WA, and Tukwila WA. Contact Lasley Orthodontics today to schedule a free consultation. Call now (425) 496-7030.

At Lasley Orthodontics, we accept most major insurance providers. However, the only way to check your insurance coverage for orthodontic treatment is by scheduling a free consultation. If your orthodontic treatment is included apart of your insurance coverage, you may receive the benefits of reduced out of pocket costs.

Finding The Best Braces Cost In Renton WA

Where Can I Find Out About Invisalign Vs. Braces Near Me?

Our team at Lasley Orthodontics is dedicated to providing braces, clear braces, Invisalign Teen, and Invisalign. As a leading orthodontist in Renton, Dr. Lasley specializes in resolving common orthodontic issues including misalignment and overcrowding.

How Does Invisalign Straighten Teeth?

The Invisalign system is designed to be invisible while being worn. Clear aligners are made of smooth and lightweight thermoplastic. Aligners are worn over your teeth for about two weeks at a time until they are replaced for the following set in the series. Invisalign is made with the patient’s needs in mind and intended to remain comfortable by gently shifting teeth into alignment.

How Do Braces Straighten Teeth?

Braces are made of stainless steel brackets that are bonded to the front of your teeth and connected together by archwires. Due to modern technology, braces have become less noticeable and more comfortable than ever before. Furthermore, clear braces provide the option of translucent brackets that blend in with your natural tooth color.

What Are The Benefits?

Every form of orthodontic treatment is intended for the same purpose, which is to achieve a straighter and healthier smile. However, Invisalign and braces both hold their own sets of benefits that may be appealing to different patients depending on their lifestyle and needs.

Invisalign

Virtually invisible Keep your treatment discreet
Removable No diet restrictions
Comfortable and durable Made of smooth and lightweight plastic

Braces

Benefits Advantages
Powerful More effective for complex issues
Fixed appliance 24/7 treatment around the clock
Cost efficient Traditional braces are often cheaper than other clear options.

Choosing Between Invisalign Vs. Braces In Renton WA

Where Can I Find Out About Invisalign Vs. Smile Direct Club Near Me?

Our team at Lasley Orthodontics specializes in Invisalign and Invisalign Teen in Renton WA. As a leading Renton orthodontist, Dr. Lasley is knowledgeable and experienced in all forms of orthodontic care. As a result, we provide supervised treatment with Invisalign by an orthodontic specialist that received the necessary education and training to help guarantee the best possible results.

What Is Invisalign?

The Invisalign system uses sets of clear aligner trays to provide the same results as traditional braces. Clear aligners are virtually invisible so no one will ever have to know about your orthodontic treatment. Each set of aligners is made of smooth and lightweight plastic to gently and gradually shift teeth into alignment. Invisalign and Invisalign Teen are custom-made with SmartTrack material for a perfect fit that will never slip out or affect your speech.

What Is Smile Direct Club?

Smile Direct Club also uses clear aligners to achieve straighter teeth. However, Smile Direct Club is designed to eliminate visits to an orthodontist by allowing treatment to be completed from home. First, you will need to locate a nearby “SmileShop” where dental impressions are taken. In addition, you also have the option of receiving a dental impression kit in the mail. Once your dental impressions are received, each set of aligners will be delivered right to your door. However, it is important to keep in mind that Smile Direct Club eliminates the supervision of an orthodontist or dental professional throughout the course of treatment.

Why Choose Invisalign With A Certified Orthodontist?

The ADA strongly discourages against do-it-yourself orthodontics because of the potential dangers or harm it can cause. It is believed that supervision by a licensed dentist or orthodontist is necessary for all phases of orthodontic treatment including oral and periodontal examinations, X-rays, treatment planning, prescriptions, periodic progress evaluations, and a stable final assessment. If the current course of treatment is not achieving the proper results, an orthodontist has the choice to switch to braces or another form of effective treatment. As a result, continuous supervision by a Parkland orthodontist such as Dr. John will ensure the health of your teeth to prevent tooth loss and any other unnecessary complications.

Finding The Difference Of Invisalign Vs. Smile Direct Club

item.content:encoded:
Choosing an orthodontic practice that you can trust with you or your smile is an important decision. Orthodontic treatment may continue over long periods of time, so you want to make sure that you are comfortable with the orthodontist and the supporting staff. An experience with a Renton orthodontist is something you will remember for a lifetime, so make sure it is something you feel comfortable about. Here are some tips on how to choose the best orthodontist in Renton:

Ask for recommendations

Selecting the right orthodontist in Renton WA may seem like a difficult task, so it’s a good idea to ask about a recommendation from someone who you trust. Whether it’s a friend or family member, it is likely they will be more than happy to share their experience with you.

Consider experience and education

Once you have an orthodontist in mind, do your research on them. Find out about their educational background, including what types of specialty training they’ve completed. Before you schedule a consultation with an orthodontist, make sure they are a licensed member of the American Association of Orthodontists. This ensures that they remain up-to-date on the newest and most effective treatments.

Get multiple consultations

Many orthodontists offer free consultations that provide an opportunity to evaluate patients and provide recommendations on procedures and services such as braces and Invisalign. This provides an opportunity for patients to meet and preview an orthodontist before receiving care. See how the orthodontist interacts with patients, and keep in mind how the staff provides care. It is essential to choose an orthodontic office that makes you feel comfortable and positive about treatment.

Ask questions

During the initial consultation, don’t be afraid to ask questions, that’s why we are here! At Lasley Orthodontics, we believe it’s important for you to understand the type of orthodontic issues you have as well as the most effective ways to treat them. The more information you know about your own dental health, the better decisions you will be able to make moving forward.

item.title: Invisalign Clear Aligners – Everything You Need To Know
item.description: What Are Invisalign Clear Aligners Made Of? Invisalign eliminates the need for bulky, uncomfortable materials such as metal, wires, and elastics. Clear aligner trays are made up of smooth thermoplastic known as SmartTrack material. This material was created to work exclusively with Invisalign treatment, which allows for better control over tooth movement. How Is Invisalign Different […]
item.content:encoded:
What Are Invisalign Clear Aligners Made Of?

Invisalign eliminates the need for bulky, uncomfortable materials such as metal, wires, and elastics. Clear aligner trays are made up of smooth thermoplastic known as SmartTrack material. This material was created to work exclusively with Invisalign treatment, which allows for better control over tooth movement.

How Is Invisalign Different From Braces?

Unlike traditional metal braces, Invisalign treatment is removable and remains clear. Aligners are made of lightweight, smooth plastic, which eliminate the use of metal brackets and uncomfortable wires. Each set of aligners is custom-made for a perfect fit. In addition, there are no diet restrictions, which means you are able to eat the foods you love, while still brushing and flossing like normal. Invisalign is designed to fit in seamlessly with your current daily lifestyle.

How Long Does Invisalign Take?

The length of Invisalign treatmentvaries for each patient and is typically based on the severity of issues being treated. As a leading orthodontist in Renton WA, Dr. Lasley determines the length of treatment based on your treatment goals and specific individual needs.

Is Invisalign Painful?

The majority of our Invisalign patients experience minor to no pain throughout their treatment. However, there may be some slight discomfort while wearing a new set of aligners for the first time. However, don’t worry, this is only a sign that your treatment is working effectively.

Are Invisalign Clear Aligners Right For Me?

Invisalign may be a great option for teens as well as adults who want a discreet way to attain straighter teeth. Both Invisalign and Invisalign Teen are capable of correcting most common orthodontic issues including misalignment, overcrowding, and irregular spacing between teeth.

Finding Out Everything You Need To Know About Invisalign Clear Aligners

item.title: How To Make Invisalign More Comfortable
item.description: The Invisalign system has become known for the distinct advantages it offers throughout treatment. Unlike traditional braces, Invisalign is lightweight and comfortable, all while remaining virtually invisible! Clear aligners are made of smooth thermoplastic known as SmartTrack material for optimum comfort. However, some patients may experience different levels of discomfort or soreness in their mouth […]
item.content:encoded:
The Invisalign system has become known for the distinct advantages it offers throughout treatment. Unlike traditional braces, Invisalign is lightweight and comfortable, all while remaining virtually invisible! Clear aligners are made of smooth thermoplastic known as SmartTrack material for optimum comfort. However, some patients may experience different levels of discomfort or soreness in their mouth when first wearing aligners. Here are some tips to make Invisalign treatment more comfortable:

Emery board

It is normal for Invisalign patients to wear a new set of aligners and experience some slight discomfort from the edges of their aligners. To resolve this problem, we recommend using an emery board, which is similar to a nail file. An emery board has a smooth side and coarse side to help smooth out edges for a more comfortable fit.

Orthodontic wax

Orthodontic wax is a clear silicone wax that is used for dental application. In addition to an emery board, orthodontic wax helps prevent and reduce irritation in your gums, tongue, or lips. Orthodontic wax is applied along the gum line where aligner edges are located. A small strip of wax is used to prevent Invisalign aligners from irritating gums.

Canker-X

Canker-X is an ointment used for the treatment canker sores and other sores in the mouth. This ointment contains benzocaine, which is a topical anesthetic that helps numb the area of application. In addition, this also acts a bandage that sticks to gums to provide hours of additional relief and comfort.

Advil

If a Renton orthodontist doesn’t prescribe any pain medication, over-the-counter pain relievers may be quite helpful in the first day of wearing new aligners. Before putting in a set of aligners for the first time, it may be beneficial to take an Advil and apply aligners at bedtime to prevent any initial discomfort. This adjustment time will provide a more comfortable fit by the time you wake up in the morning.

Biotene & XyliMelts

Invisalign may cause dry mouth in patients who do public speaking such as speeches, lectures, or business meetings. Biotene is a moisturizing spray that lubricates the inside of your mouth for additional comfort that last for hours. XyliMelts are lozenges that rest in between your cheek and gums to soothe and moisturize the mouth throughout the day or night.

Finding Out How To Make Invisalign Treatment More Comfortable In Renton WA

item.content:encoded:
Where Can I Find The Best Orthodontist In Burien WA?

Our team at Lasley Orthodontics specializes in providing patients with the best orthodontic care available. If you are ready for a healthier smile with braces or Invisalign, a leading orthodontist in Burien WA and our friendly staff are happy to help. Find out why you should schedule a free consultation at Lasley Orthodontics today!

What Does An Orthodontist Do?

Orthodontists are proficient in the diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of orthodontic problems. Their professional lives are dedicated to creating healthy, beautiful smiles for children, teens, and adults. Although straighter teeth are often desired for cosmetic purposes, they increase the functionality of biting, chewing, and speech. Orthodontic care is often part of a comprehensive oral health plan designed by an orthodontist in Burien WA.

How Does A Burien Orthodontist Provide Straighter Teeth?

Orthodontists use a variety of dental appliances to gradually shift teeth into their new positions. Because of advanced education and clinical experience, orthodontists have the knowledge and skills necessary to recommend the best kind of treatment for each patient. An orthodontist in Burien will provide straighter teeth using methods such as:

Braces

The combination of brackets, archwires, and elastics are applied to gently shift teeth into proper alignment. Braces are available with the option of metal or ceramic brackets, for a more discreet appearance.

Invisalign

The Invisalign system consists of clear aligner trays made of smooth and lightweight thermoplastic. Clear aligners are removable and virtually invisible, which makes them a popular option for many patients.

Invisalign Teen

Invisalign Teen works the same way as Invisalign for adults, except aligners feature a hidden indicator that makes an orthodontist aware if the clear aligners are being worn as prescribed to achieve the desired results.

What’s The Difference Between An Orthodontist And Dentist?

An orthodontist and dentist are very similar and they do work together as a team. However, they help improve oral health in different ways. Dentists are responsible for covering a wide range of oral health issues. Orthodontics is a special field within dentistry that focuses on specific issues such as tooth and jaw alignment. In order for patients to make the best decisions for treatment, it is important to be educated about the differences between an orthodontist and dentist. An orthodontist is a dental specialist who has been registered after completing training in facial growth and development, biology, and biomechanics. As a result, they specialize in diagnosing and treating crooked teeth and misaligned jaws for patients of all ages and conditions.

When Should I Visit An Orthodontist?

The American Association of Orthodontists recommends that every child has an orthodontic consultation by the age of seven. However, more adults are now being treated to receive healthier smiles as well. In fact, many of our patients are over the age of 21.

How Can An Orthodontist Help?

An orthodontist helps with treating common issues such as misalignment, spacing between teeth, overcrowding, bad bites, and much more. However, braces and Invisalign offer more benefits than just straighter teeth. The orthodontic treatment provided by a Burien orthodontist such as Dr. Lasley provides benefits including:

  • Improved overall health
  • More youthful appearance
  • Better chewing/digestion
  • Boost of confidence

Finding An Orthodontist Burien WA

item.title: Foods To Eat With Braces
item.description: If you have braces, it’s important to avoid certain foods that can potentially cause damage or affect orthodontic treatment. As a leading orthodontist in Renton WA, Dr. Lasley is experienced in providing successful results in many patients, no matter their current condition or budget. What Foods Can I Eat With Braces? Braces may require some […]
item.content:encoded:
If you have braces, it’s important to avoid certain foods that can potentially cause damage or affect orthodontic treatment. As a leading orthodontist in Renton WA, Dr. Lasley is experienced in providing successful results in many patients, no matter their current condition or budget.

What Foods Can I Eat With Braces?

Braces may require some patients to alter their current diet to avoid complications or damaging braces. During your first few days of having new braces, you may not feel like eating chewy foods. However, we suggest trying softer alternatives such as tender cold cuts, mashed potatoes, soup, pudding, and oatmeal are all good foods to eat with braces.

What Foods Should I Avoid With Braces?

Many patients often wonder about the best food choices with new braces. Our team at Lasley Orthodontics recommends to avoid the following while wearing braces:

  • Sugary and starchy foods – leads to the formation of plaque acid, causing tooth decay and gum disease.
  • Sticky, chewy foods like caramel and toffee – causes loose brackets and damage to wires.
  • Hard or crunchy foods including popcorn, nuts and hard candy – leads to broken braces or causes results to be less effective.

What Are Some Healthy Options To Eat With Braces?

Ice cream may be a great choice for a treat, but you still need the necessary nourishment to look and feel your best. Eating soft food doesn’t mean you have to eat unhealthy choices. Here are some suggestions for foods to eat with braces:

  • Breakfast – smoothie, pancakes, scrambled eggs
  • Morning snack – banana, yogurt
  • Lunch – soup, rice, noodles
  • Dinner – pasta, fish, mashed potatoes
  • Night-time snack – baked apples, pudding

Finding Out What Foods To Eat With Braces

Dr. Lasley and our team at Lasley Orthodontics are professionals in orthodontic care. We are here to answer any questions or concerns you may have regarding what foods to eat with braces in Renton WA, Burien WA, Maple Valley WA, Tukwila WA, and West Seattle W . item.title: How To Clean Your Invisalign Clear Aligners
item.description: Brushing and flossing, along with properly cleaning your Invisalign trays are all important parts of good oral hygiene. Many patients choose Invisalign or Invisalign Teen because of the discreet clear appearance. However, if you do not take the proper steps to keep your Invisalign aligners clean, you may experience discoloration, odor, and a buildup of bacteria. Find […]
item.content:encoded:
Brushing and flossing, along with properly cleaning your Invisalign trays are all important parts of good oral hygiene. Many patients choose Invisalign or Invisalign Teen because of the discreet clear appearance. However, if you do not take the proper steps to keep your Invisalign aligners clean, you may experience discoloration, odor, and a buildup of bacteria. Find out how to clean your Invisalign aligners the right way by following these easy tips:

Develop A Daily Routine

Start and end each day with clean teeth and aligners. Bacteria can quickly build up on the trays overnight, so it is important to maintain a set schedule that you are comfortable following. Prevent dry saliva and plaque build-up by rinsing your trays every time you take them out. Cleaning Invisalign trays with a soft-bristled toothbrush and anti-bacterial soap helps remove plaque and bacteria, as well as keeping aligners clear and undetectable.

Soak Your Aligners

The best way to ensure your Invisalign trays are as clean as possible is to soak them in a denture cleaner or Invisalign cleaning crystals. After they are finished soaking, use a toothbrush to remove any lingering plaque or food particles and then rinse the trays off.

Brush and Floss

If you put on Invisalign or Invisalign Teen with food particles stuck between your teeth, you will be trapping them inside the trays which causes discoloration and may potentially lead to cavities and other dental issues arising. Brush and floss your teeth thoroughly to make sure your aligners remain clean and clear at all times.

What To Avoid

Always keep your Invisalign aligners in a protective case to keep them free from outside contaminants and minimize your chances of misplacing them. Do not clean Invisalign with toothpaste or mouthwash, as these can cause damage to aligners. In addition, hard toothbrush bristles will scratch aligners so we recommend an extra soft brush specifically for cleaning your Invisalign trays.

Finding Out How To Clean Your Invisalign Clear Aligners

In conclusion, our team at Lasley Orthodontics are experts in orthodontic treatment. We are here to answer any questions or concerns you may have regarding Invisalign, Invisalign Teen, or how to clean your Invisalign clear aligners in Renton WA, Burien WA, West Seattle WA, Kent WA, Maple Valley WA, and Tukwila WA. item.title: Why You Should Avoid Direct-To-Consumer Aligners
item.description: There are some things better left to professionals. When considering orthodontic treatment Lasley Orthodontics wants to remind you that safety should always be your number one concern. We strongly discourage the dangers of do it yourself (DIY), direct-to-consumer aligners and at home orthodontics. Like any fine art, orthodontic practice takes years to master. Qualified orthodontists […]
item.content:encoded:
There are some things better left to professionals. When considering orthodontic treatment Lasley Orthodontics wants to remind you that safety should always be your number one concern. We strongly discourage the dangers of do it yourself (DIY), direct-to-consumer aligners and at home orthodontics. Like any fine art, orthodontic practice takes years to master. Qualified orthodontists are the only people you should be putting your trust in when it comes to your oral health. Don’t take a risk, learn the dangers of direct-to-consumer aligners and at home orthodontics to avoid trouble later on.

Get a Professional Opinion

It is the opinion of the American Association of Orthodontists that you should avoid considering mail order or direct-to-consumer aligners. DIY orthodontic treatment and other potentially hazardous at-home orthodontics may inflict harm. Many online orthodontic services are aiming their products at you. They make treatment seem easier than ever with their services. These services might lead you to believe they are safe. Sometimes operating via selfies of your mouth, or via scanned images of your teeth. You take pictures then receive aligners in your mailbox. Sounds simple, but the reality is much more unfortunate.

“Before you decide to proceed with a direct-to-consumer orthodontic company, the American Association of Orthodontists (AAO) believes there are a number of factors and questions you may want to consider. For example, in some instances, direct-to-consumer orthodontic companies do not involve the in-person evaluation and/or in-person supervision of your orthodontic treatment by an orthodontist.”

Give Your Orthodontic Health the Best Chance

It is extremely important that you move forward with an actual in-person consultation. Treatment starts from the ground up. By getting to know individual factors that are unique to your mouth, orthodontists build a relationship with you and understand your mouth. Dr. Andrew F. Lasley is a professional in all aspects of dentistry and orthodontics. Dr. Lasley has a Doctorate of Dental Medicine from Boston University. He then completed a three-year residency in orthodontics at the University of Medicine and Dentistry of New Jersey and became a certified specialist in orthodontics. By always furthering his knowledge, Dr. Lasley strategizes a plan to give you the best chance at orthodontic success.

Avoid Problems Down the Road

It is important to consider that poor orthodontic treatment can result in more problems down the road. By seeing an orthodontist in-person you can achieve the best results possible. Mail order aligners, DIY and at home orthodontics propose a danger to you. By only focusing on the movement of teeth, these treatments lack in important areas that patients should be considerate of. The overall health of your mouth is quite important, as well as diagnosing potential issues or abnormalities that can only be found through in-person consultation.

See an Orthodontist In-Person

Even though you may think you are working toward your goal through these mail order treatments, you may actually be moving farther away from it. Please familiarize yourself with some of the common areas an orthodontist can treat in person, along with different types of braces that can work for you. Schedule a consultation today, come by our Renton, WA office, or give us a call at (425) 496-7030.

item.title: Fun Things to do In and Around Renton WA
item.description: Lasley Orthodontics want to provide you more than just a great smile. We want to make you smile. item.content:encoded:
Lasley Orthodontics want to provide you more than just a great smile. We want to make you smile. Here are some fun things to do in and around Renton WA this summer for you and your family. Be sure to check them out, and explore the community we all love!

A Summer Treat

Visit Seattle Chocolate and indulge in something sure to make you smile this summer. Chocolate is not your only option here. There are Crunchy Granola Truffle Bars, Strawberry Shortcake Truffle Bars, and Pride Truffles just to name a few specialties for the summer! Sweeten up your summer by making a stop here!

Exploration

Take a moment to unwind in Lake Sammamish State Park. Here, you can take up paragliding or hang gliding. An unbelievable experience where you can take in a breathtaking aerial view of historic forts, low hills, Key Peninsula, the Olympic Mountains, and more! A permit is required before takeoff, and everyone is expected to follow rules and regulations for safety. Take to new heights and explore like never before! You can also find activities like hiking, rock climbing or wildlife watching. There are many fun things to do in and around Renton WA this summer. Learn more about this exciting location, and all its natural sites have to offer residents. Get out, and get moving! Your overall health will flourish as well as your happiness.

Riverside State Park happens to offer off-road trails for both all-terrain vehicles as well as dirt bikes. 600 acres of trail land is sure to offer plenty of fun for riders of all skill levels. Equestrians will also find solace here thanks to a horse friendly site. The Little Spokane River welcomes paddleboats, fishermen, and other outdoor enthusiasts. Within close proximity to Spokane, this spot is really a treasure for dining, shopping, and unwinding after a long day of adventure.

Shadow Lake. Check out this lake for canoeing, kayaking, or other calm water activities. A great place to relax and catch a lovely summer breeze. Kick back and forget your worries. A lesser known spot, but still one that is still sure to delight.

History

If you love classic rock, then chances are you have heard of Jimi Hendrix. Giving unforgettable experiences and living an abundantly extravagant lifestyle set this musician apart from the rest. Some claim Jimi Hendrix is the greatest guitarist to ever live. Here in Renton WA, you can check out a memorial and pay some tribute to the late, great man.

This summer take wholesome adventures. Explore some of the fun things to do in and around Renton WA. Focus on your health as a whole. Here at Lasley Orthodontics, we love Renton WA. This is the place we have built our practice, our patients are friends and family. We take pride in bringing your whole health to new levels. Orthodontic well-being starts with an initial consultation from Dr. Lasley. We have technology like Invisalign to give you a healthy, seamless smile this summer. So while you’re out smiling on all your adventures, you can take comfort in knowing that you’re doing the most to preserve your beautiful smile. Contact Lasley Orthodontics today for community Orthodontics.

item.title: 10 Tips for Braces Pain Relief
item.description: Braces are now more comfortable than ever. Receiving braces for the first and hopefully, only time however does feel different than what most patients are expecting. To alleviate any discomfort for first time orthodontic adventurers, Lasley Orthodontics has come up with a list of 10 tips for braces pain relief you can start implementing right […]
item.content:encoded:
Braces are now more comfortable than ever. Receiving braces for the first and hopefully, only time however does feel different than what most patients are expecting. To alleviate any discomfort for first time orthodontic adventurers, Lasley Orthodontics has come up with a list of 10 tips for braces pain relief you can start implementing right now.

1: A warm rinse can combat some of the pain associated with having new braces. Warmer water is able to soothe the gums and teeth. As teeth are being straightened, giving them the benefit of warmth or even salt water can go a long way in solving irritation caused by braces.

2: Dental hygiene can definitely play a role in the development of further discomfort caused by braces. By practicing the best health techniques, one is able to care for any knicks caused by braces hardware, and discomfort will be at a lower rate.

3: Eating foods that aren’t harsh on your hardware. You will want to be sure that you follow along with the guidelines for what is and isn’t acceptable to eat or drink with braces. Avoid foods that may displace or aggravate treatment with braces. Focus on eating fruits and veggies. Anything soft for a few days once treatment has been initiated.

4: Orthodontic wax is made of silicone and creates a more comfortable barrier between the soft parts of your mouth, and your metallic orthodontic equipment. By using orthodontic wax you are able to ease irritation within the mouth.

5: Orajel can be applied directly to the sore areas in the mouth. Proven to aid in the discomfort of the common toothache, this technique can also be applied to discomfort caused by braces and other hardware.

6: The all-important Advil. Advil can reduce the inflammation caused by irritation and can also dull the pain caused by adjusting teeth.

7: Much like heat can have a positive pain-relieving effect, so can ice! Applying an ice pack to sore regions of the mouth can provide a much-needed reduction of swelling in the mouth. Also, the ice can provide a rather numbing effect for the whole mouth.

8: Drink ice water. Almost like utilizing ice, in a more refreshing form.

9: Gum massage. Gently massage your gums with your fingertips in a circular motion. Being careful to avoid rubbing your inner mouth with the sides of your brackets and wires.

10: Patience. Patience is a virtue as they say. In the majority of cases, pain generally subsides on its own within a relatively quick timeframe. Simply getting used to the hardware is the objective for the initial week, or after getting your braces tightened. If you have any persistent pain that isn’t being addressed with the above 10 tips for braces pain relief. Perhaps the best thing to do is to make an appointment with Lasley Orthodontics. If you have any questions or concerns, there is never a better time to make an appointment than now!

item.title: Exploring the Benefits of Lingual Braces in Renton WA
item.description: There are a great number of wonderful options when you want to straighten your teeth. Many people are familiar with technologies such as traditional braces, as well as Invisalign clear aligners in Renton, WA. However, there is one option that many patients don’t learn much about! Lingual braces are a great form of orthodontic treatment […]
item.content:encoded:
There are a great number of wonderful options when you want to straighten your teeth. Many people are familiar with technologies such as traditional braces, as well as Invisalign clear aligners in Renton, WA. However, there is one option that many patients don’t learn much about! Lingual braces are a great form of orthodontic treatment that many have simply never heard of. This is a shame because when you start exploring the benefits of lingual braces in Renton WA, you may be surprised to learn just how effective as well as versatile this option truly is. At Lasley Orthodontics we are consistently trying to improve the lives of our patients.

Who Can Benefit From Lingual Braces?

About four million people in the United States are currently wearing braces. Over 25% of these people are adults! Not surprisingly, many adults seek out treatment with Invisalign for the added subtlety of treatment. What they might not know is that aligning teeth with lingual braces works behind the scenes, and operates basically the same way that braces do. Braces for adults don’t have to look how you may be picturing them.

Why Should One Choose Lingual Braces?

Exploring the benefits of lingual braces in Renton, WA starts by learning more about the technology itself. If you want to delve into what lingual braces are, you will learn that they are quite simple, yet well thought out. Lingual braces are crafted by first taking an initial impression of existing teeth. Then custom brackets are created using the impressions. This makes the treatment totally unique to your mouth. Once these brackets are ready they can then be custom mounted to the backs of teeth. From this point, the treatment ensues quite similarly to what one might expect from that of traditional braces. This leaves a beautiful smile where you can see your progress along the way. Lingual braces free your smile from hardware or clear aligners. These are the truly invisible option.

Interested in Lingual Braces?

Lasley Orthodontics is home to Dr. Lasley. Our practice is founded on his principle of always putting patients first. This is why we work to offer awesome and unconventional forms of treatment that can really improve patient’s lives. The benefits of lingual braces in Renton, WA are numerous. To learn more, just give us a call, stop by, or schedule an appointment. We are happy to help.

item.content:encoded:
Invisalign For All

Lasley Orthodontics proudly offers Invisalign clear aligners. Many times our patients in Renton, WA wonder; “Is Invisalign right for me?” Invisalign is a very unique form of orthodontic treatment that is suited well for patients of all ages and all conditions. Invisalign is making a splash in the world of orthodontics for good reason.

What is Invisalign?

Invisalign is a system of clear thermoplastic aligners that gradually straighten your teeth by providing gentle pressure. By utilizing small plastic components within the aligner system, there is no problem that can’t be met with this advanced technology. Invisalign aligners are a clear and easy way to straighten your teeth. These clear aligners are also approved by the FDA and contain absolutely no harsh chemicals such as BPA, BPS, or latex. Invisalign aligners are comfortable and effective.

How Long Does Invisalign Take?

Invisalign takes about 12-18 months for the average patient depending on the person. When you ask, “Is Invisalign right for me?” You should take into account whether you are comfortable with such amazing results! Invisalign is not for everyone, especially not for people who want to see results without seeing hardware.

How Much Does Invisalign Cost?

Invisalign is a cost-effective form of treatment. Patients typically see the cost of their Invisalign treatment right around the same price as braces. Insurance and other forms of payment can help lower the cost. Advancements in imaging and software have also led to greater accuracy and more efficiency when choosing Invisalign.

Where Can I Find Invisalign?

You can find Invisalign across the country. However, you will only find the best orthodontist in Renton, WA at Lasley Orthodontics. Here at Lasley Orthodontics we genuinely care about getting results and creating a treatment that works for you. Choose Invisalign from our practice and amaze yourself with the results! Contact us today at 425 496 7030.

item.description: “Can Invisalign Fix My Underbite?” Before you go any further – yes! Invisalign can fix most cases of an underbite. Schedule an initial consultation with Dr. Lasley to get a full, comprehensive plan on how to fix your underbite using Invisalign, today. What is an underbite? Put simply, an underbite is what you experience when […]
item.content:encoded:
“Can Invisalign Fix My Underbite?”

Before you go any further – yes! Invisalign can fix most cases of an underbite. Schedule an initial consultation with Dr. Lasley to get a full, comprehensive plan on how to fix your underbite using Invisalign, today.

What is an underbite?

Put simply, an underbite is what you experience when you close your mouth and your lower front teeth are in front of your upper teeth. In most cases, this is due to a problem with your lower jaw being too far forward. Our experts have fixed hundreds of underbites, and our Invisalign patients certainly have it the best. Without traditional wires and bands, Invisalign fixes underbites while creating a minimal change in lifestyle.

What problems do underbites cause?

Underbites can make it really hard to chew properly. Additionally, the under-alignment of your jaw can make speaking difficult. Further, left untreated, underbites cause your teeth to wear down more quickly. None of these are easy problems to deal with. Thankfully, Invisalign can fix your underbite. Schedule a quick consultation today to have Dr. Lasley provide you with a full Invisalign treatment plan for your underbite.

Can Invisalign Fix More Than My Underbite?

Besides an underbite, Invisalign is capable of fixing all sorts of different smiles:

  • Overbite
  • Crossbite
  • Open Bite
  • Gap Teeth
  • Crooked Teeth

As you can see, straightening your smile is specifically what Invisalign is best at. Additionally, straight teeth can prevent periodontal disease and dental decay. Likewise, a picture-perfect smile never hurt anyone’s self-confidence.

Choose The Right Invisalign Orthodontist

Dr. Lasley specializes in creating beautiful smiles. You’re just one visit away from fixing your underbite using Invisalign. Simply click here to schedule an initial consultation, and we’ll take it from there. If you still are unsure, though, here are a few more reasons why Dr. Lasley’s Orthodontics is where you should go to fix your underbite with Invisalign:

  • Flexible financing
  • Treatments for all ages
  • Affordable payment plans
  • Years of smiling, happy results
  • Experienced Invisalign Provider
  • A friendly, comfortable atmosphere
  • Up-to-date, modern technology
  • Knowledgeable, caring staff

Simply click here to schedule an initial consultation to learn how Invisalign can fix your smile, today.

item.title: Does Invisalign Cause Weight Loss?
item.description: “Does Invisalign Cause Weight Loss?” On the surface, that may appear as a silly question. How could Invisalign clear retainers cause weight loss? It certainly isn’t through the direct intervention of weight procedures, like surgery. Furthermore, Invisalign never claims to produce any sort of nutritional enzyme to aid in the digestion of food or otherwise. […]
item.content:encoded:
“Does Invisalign Cause Weight Loss?”

On the surface, that may appear as a silly question. How could Invisalign clear retainers cause weight loss? It certainly isn’t through the direct intervention of weight procedures, like surgery. Furthermore, Invisalign never claims to produce any sort of nutritional enzyme to aid in the digestion of food or otherwise. However, the question really isn’t as silly as you think. In fact, many patients report a significant loss in weight from using Invisalign – due to the restrictions of wearing the aligners, themselves.

Not The Intention

The purpose of Invisalign is not to cause weight loss in orthodontic patients. These clear aligners replace the uncomfortable traditional metal brackets and wires from braces. In addition, the “invisible” nature of the material used in Invisalign creates a massive aesthetic appeal to anyone required to give public speeches or smile frequently. For example, the lisps, hisses, and splurts of saliva that are so present with metal braces are nearly negligible in Invisalign clear aligners.

Invisalign works by gently shifting the position of your teeth simultaneously. Rotating through a set of custom-fitted clear aligners you wear. To elaborate, patients typically go through 20-30 Invisalign clear aligners for the whole treatment plan. Also worthy of note is the timeline; patients can expect to utilize their clear aligners for approximately 12-18 months, depending on the person.

Here’s Why Invisalign Causes Some Patients To Experience Weight Loss

The ultimate culprit in this whole debacle is the amount of time required for patients to keep their aligners in place. Patients must wear their Invisalign clear aligners for a minimum of 20-22 hours per day. However, retainers are removable. We encourage all of our patients at Lasley Ortho to always remove their aligners for eating, speaking at important events, or engaging in any activities that may compromise the aligners themselves. However, the simple fact that these aligners must be worn at least 20 hours per day creates a dynamic where patients often find themselves eating less as a result. This marginal psychological change can create large habitual changes – like eating less.

To schedule your first free initial consultation for Invisalign today, simply click here! We’ll take care of the rest.

item.title: How to Care for Your Braces During a Quarantine
item.description: Here are some tips for how to care for your braces during a quarantine. Caring for your braces is always essential, but now it’s more important than ever. Although we’re able to see patients for emergencies, elective non-urgent visits will resume at a later time. We’re all looking forward to getting back to relatively normal, […]
item.content:encoded:
Here are some tips for how to care for your braces during a quarantine. Caring for your braces is always essential, but now it’s more important than ever. Although we’re able to see patients for emergencies, elective non-urgent visits will resume at a later time.

We’re all looking forward to getting back to relatively normal, but until that’s possible, there are some easy ways to help keep your treatment on track.

Do You Have An Emergency?

First, we’re here for you for dental emergencies. If you experience pain, have protruding wires, or broken brackets, you may always call us at 425-496-7030.

Fortunately, orthodontic emergencies are rare, and it’s possible to solve many problems temporarily until we can see you in person. Remember to keep any loose pieces and keep them in a plastic bag or envelope so you can bring them with you at your next appointment. Then, call us to discuss the problem and discuss scheduling an appointment.

For help determining if you have an emergency, please visit our detailed information here. There, you will find tips for temporarily resolving issues like poking or loose wires and brackets.

Tips for Caring for Your Braces During Quarantine

Here are some easy tips, followed by more details.

  1. Brush and floss your teeth after every meal.
  2. Rinse with mouthwash after brushing.
  3. Avoid sticky, chewy, and hard foods.
  4. Minimize sugary drinks and foods.
  5. Continue to wear elastics, headgear, retainers, and appliances.
  6. Use topical anesthetic (such as Orabase or Ora-Gel) and orthodontic wax for mouth sores.
  7. Rinsing with salt water may also help with sores.
  8. Use orthodontic wax or a wet cotton ball for points of irritation.
  9. Call us right away if you have any emergency.

For more detailed information about which foods to avoid, please see our resource here. Plus, you might enjoy our blog post about healthy options for those with braces here.

To help you maintain the best oral hygiene, Lasley Orthodontics has provided tips here.

Brushing and flossing with braces is a little more challenging, but essential to avoid bad breath, cavities, and prevent the onset of gum disease. Today, we ask that you please pay special attention to your daily cleaning routines.

During this time, it’s also important to regularly wash your hands for 20 seconds with soap and water.

If you are running low on elastics, please let us know as it’s important to continue wearing them as much as possible.

Also, please take extra careful care cleaning and protecting your retainers and aligners like Invisalign. For questions, please contact us. We may have to adjust the length of time you wear the aligners.

We hope this answers your questions on how to care for your braces during quarantine. By following these tips, you can avoid problems and keep your treatment on track. We’re looking forward to seeing you again as soon as possible.

https://www.facebook.com/LasleyOrtho/photos/a.529182590783226/1001861863515294/?type=3&theater

We’re Here for You

Dr. Lasley and the Lasley Orthodontics team are here for you as we face these difficult times together. Please don’t hesitate to reach out if you have concerns or are considering treatment. As more of us are staying safely at home and online, it’s the perfect time to plan for a healthier future with more smiles.

Please follow us on Instagram and Facebook for the latest updates and news.

Call us at 425-496-7030 or schedule a free consultation here.

https://www.instagram.com/p/B_hoNX2iRw4/

item.title: Can Invisalign Fix My Gap Teeth?
item.description: Are you wondering, “Can Invisalign fix my gap teeth?” In short, the answer is very likely a resounding, “Yes!” However, we won’t know for sure unless we’ve examined your unique teeth. That way, we can find out a path that’s right for you and your smile. Gap teeth, also known as diastema, refers to spaces between […]
item.content:encoded:
Are you wondering, “Can Invisalign fix my gap teeth?” In short, the answer is very likely a resounding, “Yes!” However, we won’t know for sure unless we’ve examined your unique teeth. That way, we can find out a path that’s right for you and your smile.

Gap teeth, also known as diastema, refers to spaces between the teeth. Both kids and adults can have diastema. While the gap may look unattractive, a more significant concern is that gap teeth can cause serious health issues.

Spaces between teeth can lead to tooth decay and sore gums. In some cases, it can occur as a result of gum disease, and the doctor will need to treat the infection first. Nevertheless, there a wide range of options to treat gap teeth. Invisalign could be a great solution with results you will enjoy for a lifetime.

Why Choose Invisalign?

If we determine that Invisalign is the best option for you, then you’ll be one of over eight million people that have perfected their smiles using the patented clear plastic aligners.

There are many advantages to Invisalign. Here are a few:

  • Faster treatment times
  • Fewer visits to the orthodontist
  • Affordable payment options
  • Visualize your smile before you begin
  • People may not know you wear Invisalign
  • No metal wire means no abrasions to your mouth
  • Quickly take out the aligners to eat, drink, brush, and floss

As you can see, Invisalign has many advantages. However, modern braces are better, more comfortable, and more discreet than ever. At Lasley Orthodontics, Dr. Lasley welcomes patients of all ages and provides the best treatment possible for everyone. After your first complimentary consultation, he will discuss the best treatment plan for you and answer all questions.

Top Invisalign Provider in Renton, WA

Now you know the answer to the question, “Can Invisalign fix my gap teeth?” If you live in the Renton, WA area, you have access to a top Invisalign provider, Dr. Andrew Lasley, and his caring team.

At Lasley Orthodontics, we love helping the people of Renton achieve the perfect smiles they always wanted. Schedule a complimentary appointment today here or call us at 425 496 7030. We look forward to meeting you and discussing if Invisalign is right for you!

Please follow us on Instagram and Facebook for the latest updates and news.
