item.title: The Benefits of Invisalign
item.description: In this post, we’ll look at the benefits of Invisalign. The popular clear aligners can do much more than improve your smile but also your overall health. If you live in the Renton, WA area, you have access to a top Invisalign provider, Dr. Andrew Lasley, and his caring team. So, let’s look at some of the […]
item.content:encoded:
In this post, we’ll look at the benefits of Invisalign. The popular clear aligners can do much more than improve your smile but also your overall health.

If you live in the Renton, WA area, you have access to a top Invisalign provider, Dr. Andrew Lasley, and his caring team. So, let’s look at some of the top reasons to get started with Invisalign or braces.

Top Benefits of Invisalign

Orthodontic treatment with Invisalign helps you avoid health problems. Yes, you can prevent serious health issues by perfecting your smile. For example, space between your teeth can lead to decay due to particles of food trapped in the gaps. However, there are many other potential problems you can avoid.

Avoid Health Issues

Misaligned teeth, also called malocclusions, can also lead to:

  • Discomfort chewing
  • Speech problems
  • A prematurely aged appearance
  • Tooth decay and loss
  • Pain in the jaw and sometimes associated headaches
  • Receding bone in the jaw
  • Gum disease

With a treatment like Invisalign or braces, you can avoid health issues like these. Clearly, this is one of the best benefits in the long-term. As such, orthodontic treatment can change your life in more ways than one.

Find out if you’re a candidate for Invisalign by booking a free consultation today.

Convenient, Comfortable, Faster Treatment

With Invisalign, your path to a perfect smile may be shorter, with comfortable treatment. Plus, since you can take out the aligners, it’s more convenient. There’s no need to worry about brushing or flossing around braces. Just take out your aligners, and you can brush, floss, and eat as you usually would.

Combined with the latest technology, your treatment can be much faster too. In some cases, this can mean you’ll wear the aligners for months instead of a year. So, you’ll also enjoy the convenience of fewer office visits. Nevertheless, you get the same fantastic results without compromise.

Nobody Knows You’re Working on Your Smile

Another one of the benefits of Invisalign is how discreet the customized clear aligners look. Thus, you’ll be more inclined to smile during treatment. And, unless you tell them, most people won’t know you’re wearing Invisalign. For self-conscious teens, this is a lifesaver for school photos and events.

However, professionals and adults of all ages enjoy Invisalign’s appearance. Also, today’s modern braces are more discreet and streamlined than ever. So today, patients for aligners and braces can enjoy smiling more without feeling self-conscious.

item.content:encoded:

Here are some important questions to answer:

  1. How experienced is the orthodontist?
  2. Is treatment affordable?
  3. What are patients saying about their experience?
  4. Does the practice offer the latest technology?
  5. Does the orthodontist give ample time to each patient?
  6. Are the staff and doctors friendly and fun to be around?

As you can see, these are some of the important things to answer as you consider your first appointment. Importantly, finding the right match for you means selecting an orthodontist who meets your expectations for all of these things.

The Best Orthodontist in Renton, WA

item.title: Can Invisalign Fix My Overbite?
item.description: Are you wondering, “Can Invisalign fix my overbite?” Notably, an overbite is the most common malocclusion, a name for misaligned teeth. Overbites make up about 70 percent of the dental disorders in children. In fact, the majority of people have a small overbite. Many times, an overbite is slight and may not cause a problem. However, in […]
item.content:encoded:
Are you wondering, “Can Invisalign fix my overbite?” Notably, an overbite is the most common malocclusion, a name for misaligned teeth. Overbites make up about 70 percent of the dental disorders in children. In fact, the majority of people have a small overbite.

Many times, an overbite is slight and may not cause a problem. However, in some cases, the overbite is more pronounced. By seeking orthodontic treatment, you can potentially avoid serious health issues. Not to mention, you’ll look better, and often, patients have more confidence.

Invisalign for Overbites and Much More

An overbite is one of many malocclusions that Invisalign can correct. Recently, we looked at how Invisalign can help those who have gap teeth as well. Invisalign can be the best treatment for other problems like:

  • Underbites
  • Crossbites
  • Open Bites
  • Crowded teeth

Plus, Invisalign can help generally straighten your teeth, resulting in a perfect smile.

https://www.instagram.com/p/Bla5U_fnq4e/

Advantages of Invisalign

Today, it’s easier than ever to treat an overbite thanks to ever-improving technology. Invisalign is a popular option for many reasons, such as:

  • Eat what you want
  • Discreet nearly invisible appearance
  • Can be more comfortable without wires
  • Fewer abrasions to gums
  • Can be fewer office visits
  • Faster treatment times
  • Freedom to take out aligners to eat and clean teeth

Disadvantages of Invisalign

Although Invisalign has incredible advantages, there are a few challenges for some patients.

  • Must be disciplined to wear aligners at least 22 hours a day
  • Aligners must be carefully protected when they are out
  • Aligners must be kept clean, but it may be easier than with braces
  • May not be the best option for advanced malocclusions

Despite these slight disadvantages, over eight million people have enjoyed fantastic results with Invisalign. Also, the treatment continues to improve. Combined with the latest technologies, treatment can be faster and more comfortable than ever before.

Are you a candidate for Invisalign? To find out, you need an orthodontist to assess your smile.

https://www.instagram.com/p/CC3dDGIKCIM/

Why Treat an Overbite?

Consider the following issues sometimes caused by a pronounced overbite:

  • Trouble chewing food properly
  • Damage to the teeth and gums
  • Painful chewing or biting
  • Breathing issues
  • Speech impediments
  • Change to a person’s appearance

As you can see, it’s often a wise move to treat an overbite.

Choose a Top Invisalign Provider

It’s easy to find out, “Can Invisalign fix my overbite?” Contact the top provider in the Renton, WA area, Lasley Orthodontics. Dr. Andrew Lasley and his team focus on getting to know each patient and understanding their goals. Then, Dr. Lasley will design the best custom treatment plan for your unique smile.

As you steadily progress toward the smile of our dreams, we’re here for you every step of the way. Thus, treatment stays on track, and you are ready to show off your new smile in less time. Plus, we work with you to ensure treatment is affordable and within your budget, so nothing’s holding you back.

Schedule a free consultation today. Just click here to get started.

Please follow us on Instagram and Facebook for the latest updates and news.

Or, call us at 425 496 7030.

https://www.instagram.com/p/CCEjYsXp-7z/

item.title: The Cost of Invisalign
item.description: In this blog, we’ll look at the cost of Invisalign, the clear aligners that have transformed over eight million smiles. Today, Invisalign is popular with people of all ages and has improved over the years. Thus, treatment is just as attractive for adults as for teens. Everyone loves how discreet Invisalign is since most people won’t know you’re working […]
item.content:encoded:
In this blog, we’ll look at the cost of Invisalign, the clear aligners that have transformed over eight million smiles.

Today, Invisalign is popular with people of all ages and has improved over the years. Thus, treatment is just as attractive for adults as for teens. Everyone loves how discreet Invisalign is since most people won’t know you’re working on your smile.

However, the benefits of Invisalign go much further than appearance. For example, you can avoid oral health issues, looking, and feeling better. Even better, Invisalign is affordable with payment plans from Lasley Orthodontics.

So, you don’t have to have dental insurance to get affordable treatment. However, if you have insurance, our staff will help you maximize your benefits.

The Average Cost of Invisalign

Nationwide, Invisalign and braces tend to cost around the same. According to the Consumer Guide for Dentistry, Invisalign generally costs between $3K to $5K. On the other hand, braces can cost between $2 to $6K. With braces, the cost varies a bit more because there are so many types of modern braces. Also, the cost of any treatment depends on your unique smile. Thus, the cost can be more in some cases that require more extensive treatment.

Once Dr. Lasley learns about your goals and examines your smile, he’ll carefully design the best possible custom treatment plan. Before any treatment begins, you’ll discuss all the options, the exact costs involved, and affordable payment plans. Therefore, you’ll always know what to expect at all times.

Keep in mind; not everyone will be a candidate for Invisalign, although the aligners treat a wide variety of cases. Dr. Lasley’s expertise allows him to treat a wider variety of cases with Invisalign. However, modern braces could provide more effective treatment in some cases.

In all cases, the result is a perfect smile that you’ll be proud to show off for life. Below, see one of our smiling Invisalign success stories from our Instagram.

https://www.instagram.com/p/B6Q0idfpUoW/

Better Health and More Confidence

Considering all the benefits of Invisalign, not to mention the end result, Invisalign is worth it. As noted, Invisalign is more than cosmetic. Importantly, you can avoid oral health issues by straightening your smile. See more about the health benefits of orthodontic treatment in our recent blog.

As you improve your health, you can also enjoy the convenience of eating whatever you like, unlike braces. Simply take out the aligners when you eat, drink, or brush your teeth. Also, treatment can be much faster than in the past as well as more comfortable.

Thus, Invisalign provides the perfect solution for busy professionals, students, and athletes. However, you do need to wear the aligners for at least 22 hours a day.

Find out if you’re a candidate for Invisalign by scheduling a free consultation today! Just click here to get started.

When you call Lasley Orthodontics, you’re calling on one of the top Invisalign providers in the state. Thus, you can get the best custom treatment plan and achieve a perfect smile in less time.

Or, call us at 425 496 7030. We look forward to meeting you and showing you why Lasley Orthodontics is the best choice for Invisalign in the Renton, WA area.

Please follow us on Instagram and Facebook for the latest updates and news.

item.title: Braces-Friendly Foods
item.description: In this blog, we’ll look at braces-friendly foods. While you wear braces, it can take some time to adjust. Mostly, this adjustment period is after you first start wearing braces. During this time, some foods are better avoided. However, these foods can generally be added back to your diet later. Then, there are other foods […]
item.content:encoded:
In this blog, we’ll look at braces-friendly foods. While you wear braces, it can take some time to adjust. Mostly, this adjustment period is after you first start wearing braces. During this time, some foods are better avoided. However, these foods can generally be added back to your diet later. Then, there are other foods to avoid for the duration of treatment.

At first, it might seem difficult to avoid some of these foods, especially if they’re a favorite. However, most people adjust quickly, and before long, you won’t even think about it. Over time, you’ll develop new favorites and will immediately recognize which foods are out –at least until your new smile is revealed!

It’s all about keeping your treatment on track and making it comfortable. By avoiding these foods, you can ensure just that. Otherwise, you may experience a broken bracket or wire or problems with tooth decay. Therefore, sticking to the right foods will help you avoid costly and sometimes painful emergency visits.

Best of all, you can progress toward a perfect smile smoother and faster. Then, you can celebrate and indulge in those items you may or may not have missed.

Delicious, Soft Items for Well-Balanced Diet

First, we’ll look at the foods that you can enjoy while wearing braces. As for the sweeter, starchier foods, be sure to rinse, brush, and floss after eating them. However, after any meal, that’s recommended to avoid particles of food that can be trapped around brackets and wires.

Some delicious options to consider:

  • Soups
  • Seafood
  • Soft fruit
  • Pancakes
  • Muffins
  • Puddings
  • Soft bread
  • Soft cheese
  • Cooked rice
  • Milkshakes
  • Cooked pasta
  • Oats and grains
  • Soft wraps and tortillas
  • Cooked and soft veggies
  • Soft desserts and cakes

As you can see, these foods are soft and easier to chew. Among the choices, you can have a well-balanced nutritious diet, which is always essential. Before long, you will automatically consider if the food is safe and know what to avoid.

As for the sweeter treats like soft cake and ice cream, we recommend limiting sweets in general to a few times a week. However, this is a good idea with or without braces.

See more about foods that are safe with braces here.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho) on Sep 16, 2020 at 8:11am PDT

Foods to Temporarily Pause

Right after you get your braces, your teeth and gums will be more sensitive than usual. During this time, some foods may be better avoided until you have some time to adjust. Then, you can start adding them back in if you like.

Foods to avoid as you adjust to braces:

  • Spicy and citrus foods can worsen the sensitivity
  • Bread and rolls that are touch to chew or have a thick crust
  • Thicker cuts of meat that require more chewing

Another choice that may or may not be a temporary problem is ice cream. For many, ice cream can help with new braces as the cold lessens swelling. However, others will be very sensitive to cold food for a time.

Similarly, some food can stain braces. For example, coffee and tea can discolor clear brackets. So, be sure to rinse with water or mouthwash after consuming these beverages.

What to Avoid With Braces

Before you consider eating something, think, “Is this food going to stick to my braces or possibly cause damage?” Thus, it makes sense to avoid sticky, chewy, crunchy, and hard foods.

In some cases, you could cut the food into small pieces, as with apples or carrots. However, cooked carrots or apples are a safer option.

Foods That Could Be a Problem:

  • Nuts
  • Ice
  • Chips
  • Popcorn
  • Hard tacos
  • Chewing gum
  • Hard crackers
  • Corn on the cob
  • Hard or sticky candy
  • Apples, carrots, hard veggies
  • Sugary drinks, sodas, excess sweets
  • Tough bread like bagels, pretzels, or pizza crust

Also, some foods tend to stain, including beets, many berries, grapes, currants, and curry. So, these foods may be better off avoided while you wear braces.

Avoid Emergencies

Is one of these foods a favorite? If so, if might be a little hard to say goodbye for a while. However, it will be well worth it when the braces come off, and you have that perfect smile you always wanted!

By avoiding foods like these, you can ensure the day you finish treatment will come faster.

If you believe you may have an emergency, we’re here for you. Please see more about emergency appointments and tips for what to do here. If you think you may have a serious or life-threatening situation, call 911 right away.

We hope we’ve helped with this information about braces-friendly foods. Lasley Orthodontics is proud to provide the best orthodontic care in the Renton, WA area.

Schedule a free consultation today. Just click here to get started.

Or, call us at 425-496-7030.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

We’re happy to help you transform your smile and your self-confidence! item.title: Invisalign Vs. Braces
item.description: In this blog, we’ll look at Invisalign Vs. Braces. Is one treatment better than the other? What about the time it takes to get results? What are the advantages and disadvantages? Braces Are Better Than Ever Notably, there are some misconceptions about braces. Often, people worry that braces will change their appearance drastically. However, modern braces […]
item.content:encoded:
In this blog, we’ll look at Invisalign Vs. Braces. Is one treatment better than the other? What about the time it takes to get results? What are the advantages and disadvantages?

Braces Are Better Than Ever

Notably, there are some misconceptions about braces. Often, people worry that braces will change their appearance drastically. However, modern braces are much more streamlined than they used to be. Thus, the wire is harder to see, and the brackets can be clear if you choose. On the other hand, you can also choose colors if you prefer.

Due to advances in braces, they are more comfortable than ever. Plus, treatment can take less time than in the past. Generally, braces require one to three years, depending on various factors like age and how much teeth need to move.

Invisalign Vs. Braces

Now, let’s look in more detail at the pros and cons of Invisalign Vs. Braces.

Braces

The first crude form of braces dates back to ancient times. For example, Hippocrates and Aristotle discussed methods for straightening teeth as far back as 500 BC.

However, braces, as we know them, began with the use of adhesive in the 1970s. Then, dentists could bond the teeth to the brackets which hold an archwire. Early braces looked much larger, giving their wearers the dread “metal mouth” look.

Regardless of appearance, braces have always been an effective way to straighten teeth. Over the years, they continuously improved. Now, they look discreet and can rival Invisalign in that department. In particular, clear ceramic braces and lingual braces are two highly-discreet modern braces.

With braces, you do have to be more mindful about what you eat during treatment. However, there are many delicious options.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

Invisalign

Invisalign is a series of invisible, removable, and precisely fit clear aligners made just for you with 3D technology.

Critically, the thin aligners fit exactly, which means they are relatively comfortable to wear. Also, they can be taken out during treatment, one of the main differences with braces. Therefore, Invisalign allows you to eat what you like after you take out your aligners.

Notably, there are some foods best avoided with both Invisalign and braces.

Since the aligners come out, you can also brush and floss normally. Once taken out, aligners must be carefully protected and gently cleaned frequently.

Invisalign Improved Over 23 Years

Since its invention in 1997, Invisalign has continued to improve. The aligners became widely used after many years of research, marketing, and development. Today, Invisalign reports that over nine million people have benefitted from treatment.

As Invisalign improved, it has allowed orthodontists to treat more types of smiles. Also, people of all ages can now benefit.

Today, more adults are choosing Invisalign than ever. After putting off care for years, they’ve found out how convenient and effective Invisalign can be.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

A Perfect Smile Before You Know It

Although there are some adjustments to both Invisalign and braces, the benefits outweigh the temporary inconvenience. Remember, as you improve your smile, you are improving your health by avoiding oral health concerns. Before you know it, you’ll have a perfect smile to show off for life!

Both treatments can create amazing smiles. Generally, they cost about the same and may take about the same length of time. However, braces may be advisable in more serious cases. Therefore, in those cases, it makes sense treatment could be longer.

Both Invisalign and braces are affordable with payment plans from Lasley Orthodontics. If you have insurance, Lasley Orthodontics will help you file claims and paperwork to maximize benefits.

Are You a Candidate for Invisalign or Braces?

item.content:encoded:
If you’re looking for Invisalign in Renton, WA, choose a certified VIP Diamond Provider among the best in the country. Dr. Andrew Lasley is an award-winning orthodontist who loves providing the best orthodontic care to the Renton, WA, and greater Seattle areas.

When it comes to your smile, you deserve an experienced orthodontist trained in aligning the teeth and jaws. By choosing wisely, you can expect a better experience and fantastic results.

Orthodontic care is an important investment in better oral health and a future with more smiles. At Lasley Orthodontics, our entire team is committed to helping you achieve a beautiful, healthy smile. Ask us about affordable payment plans that fit your budget, so nothing holds you back.

Now is a Great Time to Begin Invisalign

Right now is a great time to start Invisalign or braces. While we’re staying at home more, you can work on your smile. Then, you’ll be ready to show off your newly aligned teeth when we return to large social gatherings again.

Today, more adults are getting the smiles they always wanted with Invisalign. Both teens and adults love how discreet, convenient, and efficient the aligners are.

Since aligners come out temporarily, you can enjoy all your favorite foods throughout treatment without the worry of damaging wires or brackets. On the other hand, there are some foods best to limit with both Invisalign and braces. Overall, one must be more mindful when wearing braces.

With braces, it also a little trickier to clean around them, but with Invisalign, you can brush normally. And, no wires means there are generally fewer appointments for adjustments. Thus, you can get the smile you dreamed of with more convenience and personal freedom.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

Invisalign Transforms Smiles and Improves Health

By getting started with Invisalign or braces, you can transform your smile and improve your oral health. Often, we see big changes in patients’ self-confidence, with more smiles than ever. All by itself, smiling can have a significant positive impact on daily life.

However, Invisalign can do much more. By aligning your teeth and jaws, orthodontic treatment can help you avoid health issues such as:

  • Tooth decay and damage
  • Periodontal disease and related problems
  • Tooth loss and underlying bone loss
  • Changes to your appearance that prematurely age you
  • Speech impediments
  • Painful bite and jaw-related problems
  • Chewing difficulty
  • Breathing issues
  • Reluctance to smile

Therefore, investing in Invisalign is well worth it, with far-reaching benefits. It’s why over nine million people and counting have completed Invisalign treatment worldwide.

Are You a Candidate for Invisalign?

item.content:encoded:
What is the best age to get Invisalign? Can adults get Invisalign, or is it only for teenagers?

Today, Invisalign has helped over 10 million people worldwide, from teens to adults of all ages. Over the years, Invisalign technology has continuously improved. Now, it can treat all kinds of smiles and get incredible results.

Both teens and adults love how discreet and convenient Invisalign can be. For example, you can enjoy your favorite foods throughout treatment. Since there’s no wire to adjust, patients generally need fewer overall appointments.

Today, more adults are getting started with Invisalign. So, there’s no “best age” to get Invisalign. If you want a beautiful, healthy, aligned smile, the best time is now.

Get started with affordable Invisalign and be ready to show your dream smile as we return to large gatherings.

Custom Treatment from a Top Invisalign Provider

At Lasley Orthodontics, patients in the Renton, WA, and greater Seattle areas get top-quality, affordable custom Invisalign treatment. Dr. Lasley is one of the top Invisalign providers in the world: A VIP Diamond Provider.

Why does this matter? This designation from Invisalign lets you know Dr. Lasley has successfully treated many patients of all ages with Invisalign. So, you can expect to get spectacular results comfortably and efficiently.

When it comes to your smile, you deserve the best orthodontist with the most experience. By choosing a top provider, you can be confident throughout treatment as you progress toward your dream smile. Not only will your smile look aesthetically beautiful, but you can enjoy better oral health for a lifetime.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

Affordable Payment Plans Make it Easy

Your smile is yours forever, and we want to make it as easy as possible for you to receive the best orthodontic care whenever you need it.

Thus, Lasley Orthodontics offers affordable payment plans. Now, you don’t need dental insurance to get affordable treatment, but our friendly staff will help you maximize benefits if you have them. We help with insurance claims and paperwork!

Before treatment, we’ll discuss payment plan options designed to fit your budget. Then, you’ll know what to expect from the beginning without surprises.

Smile More at Any Age

With orthodontic treatment, our patients of all ages smile more than ever. For example, check out our patient testimonials to see what their experiences with Invisalign were like.

As you can see, patients are smiling more than ever with more confidence. Thus, treatment can have a significant impact on daily life and well worth the investment.

The Best Age to Get Invisalign is Today

The best age to get Invisalign is today if you are living with malocclusions (misaligned teeth). Unfortunately, most people have misaligned teeth, but treatment creates perfect beautiful smiles and better health.

Today, it’s easy and free to find out if you’re an Invisalign candidate.

Just click here to get started with your FREE consultation.

When you call Lasley Orthodontics, you’re calling on one of the top Invisalign providers in the state. Thus, you can get the best custom treatment plan and achieve a perfect smile in less time.

item.title: Invisalign vs. Braces: Which is Best for Me?
item.description: Invisalign vs. braces: Which is best for me? Both Invisalign and braces are orthodontic treatments, and an orthodontist is best qualified to provide them. To determine which is best for you, you need a qualified orthodontist to assess your unique smile. Dr. Andrew Lasley provides award-winning top-quality orthodontic treatment in Renton, WA, and the greater Seattle areas. […]
item.content:encoded:
Invisalign vs. braces: Which is best for me? Both Invisalign and braces are orthodontic treatments, and an orthodontist is best qualified to provide them. To determine which is best for you, you need a qualified orthodontist to assess your unique smile.

Dr. Andrew Lasley provides award-winning top-quality orthodontic treatment in Renton, WA, and the greater Seattle areas.

The Best Custom Invisalign or Braces Available

For those who may prefer Invisalign vs. Braces, Lasley Orthodontics is among the top Invisalign providers in the world. Notably, Invisalign rates Dr. Lasley as a VIP Diamond Provider. As such, he’s treated all kinds of smiles for people of all ages. So, you can expect the best custom treatment plan, experience, and results.

Also, Dr. Lasley provides more choices in modern braces, including clear ceramic and Lingual braces that hide behind your teeth. Notably, Lasley Orthodontics offers the most advanced braces, such as the MTM No•Trace System and In-Ovation® brackets.

So, whether you choose braces or Invisalign, you can expect efficient, discreet, comfortable treatment and a beautiful, healthy smile.

Furthermore, Invisalign and braces are affordable, with payment plans to fit your budget from Lasley Orthodontics. For those with dental insurance, our friendly team will help you get the maximum benefits.

Invisalign vs. Braces

As noted, only an orthodontist can determine if Invisalign or braces will get the best results for each unique smile. Before treatment, Dr. Lasley will work with you to design a plan that fits your needs and gets the best possible results.

Today, Invisalign and braces are advanced, discreet, and can treat a wide variety of malocclusions (misalignments). As you can see here, there are all kinds of malocclusions, ranging from a common overbite to missing, crowded, and crooked teeth.

In cases where minor correction is required, Invisalign may be preferable. However, in advanced cases, braces may be the most efficient way to achieve the desired results. Importantly, Dr. Lasley’s advanced experience allows him to treat more cases with Invisalign. So, you may have a choice of treatment depending on your smile assessment.

Invisalign vs. Braces

Let’s look at some of the advantages and disadvantages of Invisalign and braces.

Pros of Invisalign:

  1. Take out the aligners to eat and brush
  2. Works for teens and adults
  3. Eat more of your favorite foods
  4. Easier to keep teeth clean
  5. Aligners are discreet
  6. Fewer appointments for adjustments
  7. No emergency visits for broken wires
  8. Comfortable precision fit

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

Cons of Invisalign:

  1. Braces might work better in some cases
  2. You must wear aligners 22 hours a day
  3. You must keep track of aligners at all times
  4. It’s best to avoid sugary, sticky, or hot foods and liquids

 

Thus, Invisalign has some great advantages in that you can be less mindful about your diet during treatment. Best of all, treatment gets amazing results and has helped over ten million people to date.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

Pros of Braces:

  1. More discreet, comfortable, and efficient than ever
  2. Works great for minor to advanced corrections
  3. Effective for all ages
  4. Choices of colors from clear to rainbow
  5. Option for Lingual braces that hide behind your teeth
  6. You can’t lose your braces as with aligners

Cons of Braces:

  1. Wires and brackets require adjustments
  2. More important to avoid certain foods that damage braces
  3. Generally a little more visible than aligners
  4. Remain attached to your teeth throughout treatment (can be both a pro and con)
  5. Harder to clean around braces

 

Today, Invisalign and braces are faster than ever, thanks to the latest technologies.

Cost for Invisalign vs. Braces

Generally, Invisalign and traditional braces tend to cost roughly the same. However, the cost varies widely depending on each unique case. See more about the average costs in our recent blog.

Overall, orthodontic treatment is a great investment and affordable at Lasley Orthodontics. Not only will you transform your smile, but you can significantly improve your health. Over a lifetime, the benefits continue to add up. Therefore, choosing to get started can be one of the best things you do for a better future with more smiles.

Invisalign vs. Braces: Find out Which is For You

Find out if you’re a candidate for Invisalign vs. Braces by scheduling a free consultation today! Just click here to get started.

When you call Lasley Orthodontics, you’re calling on one of the top Invisalign providers and orthodontists in Washington. Thus, you can get the best custom treatment plan and achieve a perfect smile in less time.

Or, call us at 425 496 7030. We look forward to meeting you and showing you why Lasley Orthodontics is the best choice for Invisalign and braces in the Renton, WA, area.

Please follow us on Instagram and Facebook for the latest updates and news.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

item.title: How Much Is Invisalign?
item.description: How much is Invisalign? We’ll look at the average cost for Invisalign, which tends to be around the same for traditional braces nationally. At Lasley Orthodontics, treatment is affordable with affordable payment plans. Our friendly team will help make sure you get the most out of any benefits you may have for those with insurance. However, you don’t need […]
item.content:encoded:
How much is Invisalign? We’ll look at the average cost for Invisalign, which tends to be around the same for traditional braces nationally.

At Lasley Orthodontics, treatment is affordable with affordable payment plans. Our friendly team will help make sure you get the most out of any benefits you may have for those with insurance. However, you don’t need insurance to get started with the orthodontic care you deserve.

Notably, Invisalign ranks Dr. Andrew Lasley as a VIP: Diamond Provider, among the top providers anywhere in Seattle and the world. What this means is he has treated all kinds of smiles for teens and adults. Thus, you can expect the best custom treatment plan and outstanding results.

Throughout treatment, you can feel confident, as Dr. Lasley closely monitors your progress to a perfect smile.

Orthodontic Treatment Cost

With any orthodontic treatment, the cost will vary widely by individual. Why? Some people need only minor adjustments, while others may need significantly more. Also, some people may require care before proceeding to orthodontic treatment, depending on their oral health.

As you would expect, a minor treatment will take less time and cost less. For those with smiles that need more time and care, the cost will tend to be higher.

In some cases, Invisalign may not be the preferred choice of orthodontic treatment. To find out, choose an orthodontist who has years of experience beyond dental school. As smile specialists, orthodontists will determine if you are a candidate for Invisalign or if another treatment, such as braces, will help you achieve a perfect smile.

Get a free consultation with Dr. Lasley, one of the Seattle area’s most experienced and highly-rated affordable orthodontists.

How Much is Invisalign?

Now, we’ll look at the answer to “How much is Invisalign?” in the United States.

As noted, Invisalign will cost about the same as traditional braces. Thus, it tends to start at $3,000 and run up to $5,000 according to US national averages. However, as previously noted, the actual cost can vary significantly by individual needs.

According to Invisalign, the cost can vary between $3,400 and $7,100.

If you have dental insurance, it may cover a portion of the cost. For example, Invisalign suggests coverage between $1,600 and $2,300. However, the actual benefits vary widely depending on insurance policies. Always, we’ll help you get the most from any benefits and help file the paperwork.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

An Investment in Better Health and More Smiles – for Life!

As you can see, Invisalign or braces is an investment. However, the many benefits make it highly worth the cost over a lifetime.

For example, you can avoid many oral health issues as orthodontic care properly aligns your teeth and jaws.

Avoid oral health issues like:

  • Tooth decay and wear
  • Losing a tooth and underlying bone
  • Negative changes to appearance and function
  • Chewing difficulty and pain
  • Jaw pain and TMJ symptoms
  • Worsening malocclusions (misalignments)
  • Gum disease and related health issues

Additionally, many people are reluctant to show their smiles before treatment. However, following Invisalign or braces, they are smiling more than ever. All by itself, this is a big benefit and one we love about orthodontic treatment.

Today, more adults are seeking treatment and getting amazing results with Invisalign and braces. See more about the differences between Invisalign and braces in our recent blog.

Please, check out our testimonials to see how treatment can transform smiles and improve lives.

How Much is Invisalign? Get the Details for FREE.

We hope this information about “How much is Invisalign?” has been helpful as you consider treatment.

Find out if you’re a candidate for Invisalign vs. Braces by scheduling a free consultation today! Just click here to get started.

When you call Lasley Orthodontics, you’re calling on one of the top Invisalign providers and orthodontists in Washington. Thus, you can get the best custom treatment plan and achieve a perfect smile in less time.

Or, call us at 425 496 7030. We look forward to meeting you and showing you why Lasley Orthodontics is the best choice for Invisalign and braces in the greater Seattle area.

Please follow us on Instagram and Facebook for the latest updates and news.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

item.title: Brushing Techniques to Practice In Your Downtime
item.description: This time, we’re looking at brushing techniques in the Lasley Orthodontics blog. Dr. Andrew Lasley and the friendly team are committed to helping people achieve a beautiful, healthy smile from age seven to adults. If you live in the Renton, West Seattle, or greater Seattle area, choose Lasley Orthodontics for a healthy, beautifully aligned smile. When it comes to […]
item.content:encoded:
This time, we’re looking at brushing techniques in the Lasley Orthodontics blog.

Dr. Andrew Lasley and the friendly team are committed to helping people achieve a beautiful, healthy smile from age seven to adults. If you live in the Renton, West Seattle, or greater Seattle area, choose Lasley Orthodontics for a healthy, beautifully aligned smile.

When it comes to orthodontic care, you can help make your experience better and get results faster. One way to do that is by practicing a daily oral hygiene routine at home. By using the best brushing techniques, you will remove plaque and avoid tooth decay. When wearing braces, this is particularly important since particles of food can get trapped around brackets and wires.

Although most people brush their teeth daily, they may not be practicing the best brushing techniques. Also, they might not be doing it for enough time. So, it’s a good thing to practice whenever you get some downtime.

The Best Brushing Techniques

Brushing your teeth twice daily can really improve your oral health. For example, one survey found that those who brush twice a day are 22% more likely to report good oral health.

On the other hand, you can overdo it. If you brush too hard or use a brush with firm bristles, you can wear down the tooth structure over time. Therefore, it’s best to do it for the right length of time with the right toothbrush.

Brushing for Two Minutes Twice a Day

According to surveys, as many as 30% of Americans may not be brushing for quite enough time. Fortunately, about 69% of people are brushing twice a day as recommended by the American Dental Association (ADA).

To get enough time, the ADA suggests brushing for about two minutes. It’s the right amount of time to brush away plaque and food particles. However, it’s best not to brush for much longer, as it can slowly wear on tooth enamel. That’s why a soft bristle brush is always recommended. Plus, the softer bristles bend to get below the gum line.

Know the Right Brushing Techniques

For the best brushing techniques, a video is helpful so you can see it in action. Below, see a helpful video from the American Dental Association.

To summarize the proper brushing techniques:

  • Hold a soft toothbrush at a 45º angle to your gums.
  • Use ADA-approved toothpaste containing fluoride.
  • Use gentle pressure to brush back and forth in short strokes.
  • Hold the brush vertically to brush behind your teeth in short up-and-down strokes.
  • After brushing all surfaces, brush your tongue to remove plaque and bacteria.

If you have braces, be sure to brush around the brackets and wires to remove trapped particles.

Change Your Brush Frequently

Although your toothbrush might look perfect to your eye, the bristles will wear down over time. So, it’s best to throw out your old toothbrush after 3 to 4 months. Otherwise, it won’t be as effective at removing plaque.

If you have ab electronic toothbrushes, you may need to change the brush head every 12 weeks or so. Since the bristles rotate and pulse, they can wear down faster.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

Electronic Toothbrushes for Timed Brushing

With electronic toothbrushes, built-in timers help you know you’ve brushed for two minutes. Also, ADA-approved models will apply just the right amount of force, so you can’t overdo it. Furthermore, they can help those with limited manual dexterity since the rotating brush heads do much of the work.

If you prefer a manual toothbrush, it’s easy to set a timer. For kids, this can help make sure they’ve reached the two-minute goal. Then, they can mark off their completed task on a calendar. At the end of the month, parents can offer a small reward to keep it fun.

In addition to brushing twice daily, it’s best to floss once a day. That way, you can remove the plaque trapped between your teeth that brushing misses. For more on flossing and brushing with braces, see our information here. For tips on brushing with Invisalign, see our recent blog.

We hope this information on practicing brushing techniques is helpful. Questions? We’re here for you at 425.496.7030, or send us a message here.

Lasley Orthodontics loves helping people of all ages in the Renton and greater Seattle area achieve their dream smiles. Get started with a free consultation today.

Please follow us on Instagram and Facebook for the latest updates and news.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

item.title: How to Floss with Braces
item.description: When you get new braces, learning new oral hygiene practices can be time-consuming at first. Once you learn how to floss with braces it does get easier. Even though you are already adept at flossing, learning how to thread floss around the wires on your teeth can be tricky. The technician gave you a brief […]
item.content:encoded:
When you get new braces, learning new oral hygiene practices can be time-consuming at first. Once you learn how to floss with braces it does get easier. Even though you are already adept at flossing, learning how to thread floss around the wires on your teeth can be tricky.

The technician gave you a brief how-to demonstration when you got your braces, but we know that many people don’t pay much attention. Your mouth was sore. You were probably tired from the installation procedure. Everything is so new. We understand, so we created this guide with step-by-step instructions to help.

The Importance of Flossing with Braces

Floss, floss, floss. It seems like everyone is always talking about how important it is to floss. Unfortunately, they often skip the part about WHY it is important to floss, especially with braces. Understanding why it is so important often makes the chore of flossing less of a burden.

Flossing normally takes a few minutes without braces. With braces, at least at first, it may take 10 to 15 minutes. Once you get familiar with the procedure, your speed will improve.

The main reason that flossing is important is that it promotes healthy teeth and gums. Braces may hold food, including sugars and liquids. Because of the brackets and wires, if those food and drink particles are not removed they could accelerate cavity growth and gum disease.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

Not flossing enough can also cause bleeding gums which can lead to tenderness of the tissue and pain. Nobody likes a sore mouth, right? Flossing should be done at least once per day. Although we would love our patients to floss after every meal, we understand that isn’t a reasonable request. An interproximal brush can be useful to clean under wires and around brackets.

Handy Tools to Floss with Braces

Just like maintaining a bicycle or a car, it is a much easier task if you have the correct tools. For flossing with braces, there are several handy tools that you should add to your tool kit:

  • Floss threaders and floss
  • Interproximal (or interdental) brush
  • Dental tape (optional substitute for floss)
  • Toothpicks

You should also have a good toothbrush and a good, non-whitening toothpaste available.

Floss is self-explanatory. An interdental brush is a tiny brush that can slide along your brackets under the archwire to dislodge stubborn food particles. Likewise with toothpicks. Although you don’t want to be too aggressive with a toothpick, they can be instrumental in keeping your teeth, brackets, and wires clean.

Making a Portable Care Kit

Just as important as flossing is taking proper care of your appliances. Although our recommendations here are looking at general-purpose care, they also include flossing supplies. First, find a small bag or pouch to keep your kit clean and dry. A quart-sized plastic zipper bag will work in a pinch. Next, assemble the following:

  • Spare floss and floss threaders
  • Toothpicks
  • Interdental brush
  • Dental wax
  • Small, clean nail clippers
  • Clean tweezers
  • Wooden pencil with clean, unused eraser

As you go about your daily routine, having this tiny tool kit in your backpack, briefcase, or purse can be a lifesaver!

Step-By-Step Guide — How to Floss with Braces

With normal dental flossing, it is fairly easy to thread the floss between your teeth and gently work it down to the gum line. When you have traditional metal braces or clear braces, the archwire stops the floss from reaching the gum line.

Although threading the floss below the archwire is the solution, the threading can be a problem. Enter the handy-dandy floss threader! This magical little tool makes it much easier to thread floss beneath the archwire to allow flossing to the gum line. It works similarly to your grandma’s sewing needle threader.

Step-By-Step Instructions for Using a Floss Threader

Start with a piece of floss approximately 18 to 24 inches long.

  • Thread the floss strand through the floss threader, leaving a short end at around 5 inches
  • Insert the threader under your archwire, guiding it to the space between two teeth
  • Remove the threader (leaving the floss) and floss from the archwire to your gum line
  • Be sure to floss along the edges of all teeth
  • Rethread the floss threader and repeat to floss between all your teeth

For the spaces from the archwire to the tops of your teeth, you can floss normally or use a pre-threaded floss stick.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

Floss threaders are available at most retail outlets that sell toothbrushes. They are inexpensive and disposable. Use a new threading tool each time you floss. Once you have finished flossing, dispose of it properly. Once you get used to using a floss threader, the time it takes to floss with braces will grow shorter.

Using a Water Flossing Device

Water flossing devices use a directed stream of water to flush debris from between teeth and along the gum line. Although using a Waterpik® or similar water flossing device may seem like an easy way out, we should note that it is not a substitute for flossing. Rather, using a water flossing device should be considered a supplement to regular manual flossing.

How to Use a Water Flossing Device

Water flossing devices work similarly regardless of the brand you have. You may need slight adjustments to the following instructions:

  • Fill the water reservoir with lukewarm water
  • Insert the tip into the spray handle
  • Begin with low pressure, place the tip in your mouth (lean over the sink here… trust us)
  • Turn the device on, with your lips closed around the tip to prevent splashing
  • Work the tip around inside your mouth, aiming at the gum line
  • Adjust the water pressure as necessary to remove debris
  • Remove and clean the tip when finished and turn the machine off

Although water flossers are a very effective tool, they are not a replacement for regular flossing with braces.

Contact Lasley Orthodontics if You Have Questions

If you have additional questions about how to floss with braces, please don’t hesitate to contact our office at Lasley Orthodontics. Our entire staff is always eager to assist patients with questions, problems, or to offer tips and tricks.

Don’t forget to like our pages on Facebook and Instagram for updates and tips on dental and orthodontic care.

item.title: How to Not Show Gums When Smiling
item.description: The surest way to not show gums when smiling is to pretend that you are Humphrey Bogart. Anyone familiar with the film star knew of his seemingly paralyzed upper lip. While there are several stories on how the star obtained the scar that caused his upper lip to remain semi-rigid while speaking, the star was […]
item.content:encoded:
The surest way to not show gums when smiling is to pretend that you are Humphrey Bogart. Anyone familiar with the film star knew of his seemingly paralyzed upper lip. While there are several stories on how the star obtained the scar that caused his upper lip to remain semi-rigid while speaking, the star was always quiet.

One rumor says he received the scar when the Navy vessel he was serving on was shelled. That never happened. Another says he was transporting a prisoner who escaped and hit him across the face with a handcuff to cause the scar. That probably didn’t happen either and several people think the rumor was made up by the studio.

There are no records of the scar on Bogart’s official Navy discharge documentation.

The most plausible story is one from Bogart’s childhood. Apparently, the young Bogart had a wood sliver stuck in his lip. Left untreated, infection followed. The scar is supposedly the result of a doctor who, while removing the sliver, did extensive damage to the tissue, causing the scar.

Whatever the reason for Bogart’s distinctive speaking style, the one surety is that he never displayed a gummy smile.

Exploring the Gummy Smile

A smile that exposes too much gum tissue is often called a gummy smile. The technical term is a gingival smile. In many respects, whether or not this type of smile is a problem rests in the eye of the beholder. Many people don’t see it as a problem, while others don’t like the look.

According to the American Academy of Medical Orthodontics (AAMO), the definition of a gingival smile is subjective. The determination is based on a person’s perception rather than any defined medical determination. The basic definition is that too much gum is visible above the teeth.

 

View this post on Instagram

 

A post shared by Lasley Orthodontics (@lasleyortho)

According to studies, a smile displaying more than 2 to 3mm of gum tissue is not “attractive.” Although quite common. there is a higher occurrence among women than men. A gummy smile often creates the disproportionate appearance of small teeth even when tooth size is normal.

When we consider the aesthetics of a smile our view includes the lips, teeth, shape of the jaw, the alignment of the bite, and the visibility of the gumline.

Gummy smiles are not considered a medical or health condition. There may be a problem with jaw alignment if top teeth create an underbite. Fortunately, gingival smiles are completely correctable, often with non-invasive measures.

Causes of a Gingival Smile

There are many suspected causes of a gingival smile:

  • A short upper lip
  • Small teeth due to genetics
  • Teeth not fully erupted
  • Hypermobility of facial muscles controlling the upper lip
  • Gingival hypertrophy (enlarged gums)
  • Vertical maxillary excess (VME) (overgrowth of the upper jaw)
  • Gingival hyperplasia (caused by medications)

Most commonly thought to be genetic in nature, sometimes a gingival smile can be a side effect of medications.

The types of medications that may cause gingival hyperplasia treat conditions such as high blood pressure, seizures, and immune system suppressors. These medications cause an overgrowth of gingival tissue, also called clinical overgrowth. This condition can lead to a greater risk of periodontal disease if left untreated.

How to Fix a Gummy Smile

In some instances, a gummy smile will ebb on its own as a person ages. However, not everyone wants to wait that long to see if it will, indeed, just go away. For those people, there are several options they can discuss with their dentist or orthodontist.

Gingivectomy (Contouring)

This is considered minor oral surgery and it is usually performed by a periodontist. The process begins by contouring the edges of the excess gum tissue using a scalpel or laser. This exposes more of the tooth surface. When teeth do not fully erupt, crown-lengthening surgery may be necessary to expose more of the teeth.

Botulinum Toxin

Botulinum toxin, or Botox, can alter the movement of hypermobile facial muscles. When the muscles controlling the upper are hypermobile, it can cause the lip to raise higher exposing excessive gum tissue. Used in small doses, Botox can freeze, or paralyze hypermobile muscles offering the appearance of a less gummy smile.

One of the primary problems with Botox is that it is not a permanent solution. The procedure would need to be repeated every three to six months to maintain consistency in appearance.

Crowns and Veneers

One of the ways to not show gums when smiling is by having crowns or veneers installed. While crowns and veneers can be useful in improving aesthetics, this “shortcut” is usually not a recommendation. It will provide a more balanced look but could have undesired effects as the patient ages.

Orthodontic Treatments

In many instances of a gummy smile, traditional braces or Invisalign can be useful in creating a better smile. When the gingival smile is the result of an orthodontic problem, such as inward leaning front teeth, alignment will adjust the smile to decrease the visibility of the gums.

How Braces and Invisalign Work with a Gingival Smile

If a gingival smile is the result of an orthodontic issue, braces and Invisalign are both potential solutions.

Braces work by using tension to create force. The doctor affixes brackets to each tooth then threads the archwire through the brackets. When the wire is tightened it creates tension. By applying the tension, the doctor can guide tooth movement to align the smile.

Invisalign works in a similar manner but without the brackets and wires. Using custom-fit tray aligners, gentle pressure moves teeth. Patients progress through a series of sequential tray sets, with each set of trays shifting teeth closer to the goal of alignment.

Although neither braces nor Invisalign reduces the amount of gum tissue, the act of aligning the teeth broadens the smile. This wider smile and improved smile arc often reduce the amount of visible gum tissue.

Discuss Your Options with Lasley Orthodontics

If you are not satisfied with the appearance of your smile you should discuss the matter with Lasley Orthodontics. Whether you simply want to know how to not show gums when smiling or you have a different orthodontic problem, Dr. Andrew Lasley can offer solutions.

To find out how to not show gums when smiling or to discuss discovering your dream smile, request your Complimentary Examination today. You may also speak directly to our Appointment Coordinator by calling (425) 496-7030.

Visit us on Facebook and Instagram too.

item.title: Foods to Avoid with Braces This Holiday Season
item.description: You already have a list of foods to avoid with braces. Unfortunately, there are some additional foods to avoid with braces this holiday season. Don’t worry — you can still have pumpkin pie and most of the cookies. Just steer clear of that ominous pecan pie and the candy canes. The Standard List of Foods […]
item.content:encoded:
You already have a list of foods to avoid with braces. Unfortunately, there are some additional foods to avoid with braces this holiday season. Don’t worry — you can still have pumpkin pie and most of the cookies. Just steer clear of that ominous pecan pie and the candy canes.

The Standard List of Foods to Avoid

Your diet is somewhat restricted with braces, but you won’t starve. The standard list of foods to avoid includes:

Chewy Foods

These include bagels, crusty rolls, licorice, taffy, gumdrops, and similar foods. Any foods that may stick to your appliances or foods that force you to apply added pressure to chewing. You should not be using your teeth to pull or tug on any food either.

Crunchy Foods

This is the part of the list most people dislike. It includes popcorn, potato chips, and chewing on ice. Popcorn is obvious — the kernels can get caught in the brackets and wires or stuck below your gumline. Potato chips and other types of crunchy snacks tend to shatter when you bite them. The little bits can also get stuck in your appliance or cause tiny cuts and abrasions in your mouth.

Sticky Foods

Anything that can stick to your appliances or your teeth can cause problems. Caramels and chewing gum may be awesome treats, but while chewing on them, the sugary candy may stick to your braces. The effort may also cause your teeth to shift slightly which could delay your treatment.

Hard Foods

Hard candies and nuts can cause your brackets to dislodge or break your archwire. If you can suck on hard candies and dissolve them rather than biting down on them, you can occasionally indulge. Otherwise, you should avoid nuts and hard candies.

Foods That You Bite Into

This category includes corn on the cob and eating a whole apple. It can also include sub sandwiches on crusty rolls, pizza, and other foods that require pulling or tugging with your teeth. You don’t always have to avoid these foods — you just have to eat them differently.

Cut the corn off the cob. Peel and dice your apple into bite-sized pieces. Cut your pizza into bite-sized pieces. The trick is to avoid pulling and tugging. With bite-sized pieces, you can use your molars to chew these types of foods. Although there is still a slight risk and you’ll have to chew carefully to avoid damaging your braces, changing how you eat the foods in this category allows you to continue eating them.

Focus on the Types of Foods

Rather than trying to memorize a list of specific foods, we ask that you just get used to thinking about what you are eating. Because the biggest reason for avoiding certain types of food is to protect your braces from damage, consider whether or not something might cause damage.

Keeping a simple question in mind will help. Ask yourself, “Will this damage my braces?” If the answer is yes, that food should be avoided. If the answer is no, then you can eat it. On the other hand, if the answer is maybe, consider what you can do to make it safer to eat with your braces.

As we discussed in the previous section, how you eat foods can make a difference. Chomping into a whole apple can possibly cause your brackets to loosen or pop off and may break the archwire. The peels can get stuck in the wires and be difficult to remove. However, peeling and dicing the apple into half-inch pieces allows you to eat it in a safe manner, using your molars rather than biting into the fruit.

Foods to Avoid with Braces This Holiday Season

Many families keep a bowl of nuts out during the holidays. The nutcracker was always handy and these were a great snack between meals that parents seldom complained about. Unfortunately, nuts can dislodge your brackets or break the archwire on your braces.

Unfortunately, avoiding nuts means avoiding that delicious pecan pie and any of Aunt Susie’s cookies that have nuts in them.

Likewise with hard candy. Maybe Grandma always has a bowl of rock-hard ribbon candy or peppermint drops out throughout the year, or maybe just during the holiday season. Either way, you should avoid the temptation.

Foods You CAN Eat This Holiday Season

You can still eat all the same foods that you have been eating. Adding to that list are the holiday-specific treats. You can eat ALL the pumpkin pie, but sharing is still nice.

The key to staying safe during the holidays means being conscious of what you are eating. As long as you remember to ask if something will be harmful to your braces, you can enjoy a wonderful holiday season filled with sweet treats and festive times!

General Oral Care for Your Braces

Remember to carry your portable care kit when you visit friends and relatives. You will undoubtedly be eating more sweets which means you will want to step up your game on oral hygiene. Remember to brush and floss regularly throughout the season. Whether you are at a family gathering or watching a bowl game with your friends, oral care is an important part of keeping your treatment on schedule.

What to Do If You a Have a Braces Emergency

The first step if you do have a braces emergency is to call Lasley Orthodontics. Your teeth may feel a little loose with braces. This is a normal part of the treatment as your teeth shift into new positions. It is also why you need to avoid those hard candies and cookies with nuts.

If you have a loose wire, band, or bracket, you can secure it with a pea-sized piece of dental wax. This will keep it from being damaged further until you can get to the office for repair. If a part of the appliance becomes completely removed, save it and bring it with you to the office.

For broken wires that may be poking the inside of your mouth, you can bend them away from your soft tissue with the back of a spoon or the eraser on a new pencil. Make sure to add a dab of dental wax to stabilize the wire and keep it from cutting your mouth.

Taking care of your braces is a part of the process. If you wear headgear at night and spend the night at a friend’s house during your school break, please take your gear. It may seem embarrassing, but it will keep your treatment schedule on track.

Contact Lasley Orthodontics if You Have Questions

If you still have questions about what foods to avoid with braces this holiday season, contact Lasley Orthodontics. Our staff will be on hand to help. If you don’t need an immediate answer, you can use the online contact form. To speak with staff, please don’t hesitate to call us at (425) 496-7030.

Have a Happy Holiday Season!!

item.title: Why Do Teeth Shift
item.description: The environment inside our mouths is constantly changing. The mouth is the main gateway into the body for food, water, and air. It is only sensible to believe that this environment will undergo many changes throughout your life. One of these significant changes is teeth shifting, or teeth changing positions. But why do teeth shift? […]
item.content:encoded:
The environment inside our mouths is constantly changing. The mouth is the main gateway into the body for food, water, and air. It is only sensible to believe that this environment will undergo many changes throughout your life. One of these significant changes is teeth shifting, or teeth changing positions. But why do teeth shift? Is it a disease, or is it a normal occurrence? What can you do to stop that shifting? We will answer all of that as your read on.

What Is Meant by Teeth Shifting?

Teeth shifting describes your teeth leaving their normal positions and starting to drift. Depending on many factors, this shift could be forwards, backward, upwards, or downwards.

Typically, our teeth are held firm in their position in the bone by a bunch of fibers known as the periodontal ligament (PDL). The PDL acts as a cushion, absorbing all the forces from chewing and biting and ensuring the teeth remain in their position without shifting or any other problems. When the forces become too large for the PDL to withstand, that’s when the shifting could start.

Why Do Teeth Shift?

The mechanism behind teeth shifting is an interesting one. The forces inside our mouths, from eating, chewing, and even speech, always tend to make the teeth move around and shift. However, the bone and PDL are strong enough to counteract these forces. Not only that but all the teeth are joined together in spots known as contact areas. In addition, they are always opposed by the teeth opposite them in the other jaw. This whole assembly makes the teeth stand firm in their place. When you understand the problems that could happen to any of these structures, you’ll begin to understand why do teeth shift.

Is It Normal for Teeth to Shift?

There are two types of shifting, pathological and normal aging shifting.

Pathological Shifting:

This happens when the shifting results from a problem or disease inside the mouth. Examples of these problems include:

    1. Teeth loss:
      As we explained, the teeth help each other stand firm in their place. So if one tooth is lost, the teeth next to it start to drift into that space, and the tooth opposing it also starts to elongate since there is nothing to stop it. This is why you should seek to replace any missing tooth as soon as possible.
    2. Decay:
      Small decays don’t really affect teeth’ positions, but when the decay becomes large enough to destroy the contact areas, the support from the remaining teeth is lost, and the teeth would start to move around.
    3. Periodontal disease:
      As we explained, the PDL is the main structure that keeps the teeth attached to the bone. If the PDL is diseased, the teeth will lose their support. Periodontal disease often starts simple with some gum inflammation but can progress rapidly into a cascade of events that could eventually lead to tooth loss.
    4. Not wearing your retainer after braces:
      This is not a disease, strictly speaking. However, the mechanism is the same and should be considered as such. Your retainer is a crucial piece of your treatment with braces. After you’re finished with braces, your bones and PDL are still unstable from all the changes. The retainer would help stabilize them until they are accustomed to the new teeth’ positions. If you do not wear your retainer, you give them the chance to revert to their original state, and you would find yourself exactly where you started with crooked, crowded, or spaced teeth.

Normal Aging Shifting:

Two phases in life must be recognized as normal phases for teeth shifting:

    1. Late teen shifting:
      This is also known as late teen crowding. Often at 16 or 17, some patients may complain that their teeth shifted, especially the front teeth. They would complain that the teeth were positioned normally but then started to get out of place with no apparent reason. It is thought that the emergence of the wisdom tooth is one of the reasons behind such shifting, but why do teeth shift at this age exactly is not yet fully understood.
    2. Old age shifting:
      With age, a number of changes can happen in the mouth. The teeth start to wear away from years of service, and the bones become more fragile and brittle. Add that to all the dental work that was probably done during your lifetime, and you’ll understand why teeth shift in old age.

Do Teeth Eventually Stop Shifting?

The aging shifting usually does not stop. However, it is usually very slowly progressing and can be controlled with the help of your dentist. The pathological shifting would stop as soon as the reason behind the shifting is removed.

How Do You Stop Teeth Shifting?

It all comes down to the removal of the cause of the problem. If you have a missing tooth, seek to replace it as soon as possible with implants, bridges, or dentures. If you have severely decayed teeth, you should strive to fix these as quickly as possible. Periodontal disease is easier to prevent than cure. If you frequent your dentist or hygienist and take good care of your oral hygiene, you can do that.

Finally – and we cannot stress this enough – keep wearing your retainer at the times assigned by your orthodontist until he deems it OK for you to remove it. Think of it as part of your treatment. You endured braces for a year or more in the hopes of changing your appearance, so what’re a few more months?

If you need more help in understanding why do teeth shift, Please don’t hesitate to contact Dr. Andrew Lasley and the team at Lasley orthodontics. Our team is always here for you to answer any questions regarding braces, Invisalign, Invisalign Teen, or to know if you even need braces. You can find us at 16655 108th Ave SE, Renton, WA 98055, or give us a call to schedule your complimentary consultation at (425) 496-7030.

item.title: Can Adults Get Braces Too?
item.description: Everybody loves to grin, a quiet infectious spread. However, reluctant shame conceals a lovely grin for some who don’t like their teeth. If that describes you, you might be wondering, “Can Adults Get Braces too?” Adults in Renton, Washington, consider braces for a variety of reasons. As a teenager, the access or need for braces might […]
item.content:encoded:
Everybody loves to grin, a quiet infectious spread. However, reluctant shame conceals a lovely grin for some who don’t like their teeth.

If that describes you, you might be wondering, “Can Adults Get Braces too?”

Adults in Renton, Washington, consider braces for a variety of reasons.

As a teenager, the access or need for braces might not have been available; your oral well-being needs might have changed, or maybe you didn’t understand that adults can get their teeth fixed.

Presently, there are more choices than at any other time in recent memory for grown-ups to develop perfect smiles.

Treatments are more discreet than the metal contraptions you might keep in mind from school. They’re lighter, more comfortable, and more cost-effective. Back-of-the-teeth orthodontics, clear or tooth-hued clay and porcelain, and clear aligners (for example, Invisalign-the most exceptional clear aligner on the planet) are only a few well-known choices to get your best grin 50% quicker.

 

What Are Adult Orthodontic Treatments Available?

We believe that every smile is unique, and we at Lasley Orthodontics offer various orthodontic treatment options, from braces to Invisalign.

Metal Braces:

The most well-known teeth-straightening orthodontic choice out there continues to be metal braces. These suitable braces have become increasingly proficient and more agreeable than in recent memory.

Lingual Braces:

Lingual braces are placed on the tongue side of the teeth, making them less noticeable than traditional braces. They function similarly to metal braces, but no metal is visible because your corrective structure sits behind the teeth.

Lingual braces aren’t appropriate for every orthodontic issue, and they can be more expensive than traditional braces.

Invisalign:

The freshest teeth-straightening strategy available, Invisalign uses a progression of transparent plates called aligners. These are shaped to custom fit your mouth and change the arrangement of your teeth – no sections or wires required.

You can undoubtedly maintain excellent oral cleanliness and even sparingly eliminate the aligners for significant occasions.

Why is grown-up orthodontics more popular than any time in recent memory?

It’s simple; a perfect smile is more obtainable than ever. Innovations in orthodontics continue to improve, making it faster than ever to have a fantastic smile.

 

Contact Dr. Andrew Lasley and Lasley Orthodontics Team to answer any questions regarding Braces, Invisalign, and Invisalign Teen.

You can find us at 16655 108th Ave SE, Renton, WA 98055, or give us a call to schedule your complimentary consultation at (425) 496-7030.

item.title: Life After Braces: Maintaining Your Beautiful Smile Post-Treatment
item.description: Congratulations on finishing your braces treatment in Renton WA! In this quick read, we’ll explain what to expect in your new life after braces. As well as helpful tips on how to maintain your new and improved smile. What to Expect on Your New Life After Braces Having your braces removed is such a lovely […]
item.content:encoded:
Congratulations on finishing your braces treatment in Renton WA! In this quick read, we’ll explain what to expect in your new life after braces. As well as helpful tips on how to maintain your new and improved smile.

What to Expect on Your New Life After Braces

Having your braces removed is such a lovely feeling. That being said, here are some things to look forward to in your life after braces.

Retainers

Getting your braces taken off is only just the beginning of the healing process. You’ll be transitioning into the retention phase afterward. Proper retainer wear is important in order to ensure that your teeth won’t shift and remain there for a beautiful smile.

Teeth or gum soreness

Soreness after the removal of your braces is a relatively common occurrence. Many patients feel it for a few days, especially on the day of the extraction. An orthodontist may use pressure in order to ensure all brackets and sealant are intact while they’re removed from your teeth. The soreness will subside after a couple of days or a week. So, it’s not something to worry about.

Brushing and flossing

You’ll notice how easier it is to brush and floss without all the metal and elastics. While it is more convenient to clean your teeth and gums, you still need to keep up your daily cleaning habits.

Calluses

It’s possible to have calluses on the inside of your mouth due to the braces rubbing against them for quite some time. It’s easy not to feel them while wearing braces, but after they are removed, you’re likely to notice them more. But this isn’t a major cause for concern since they will go away over time.

Discoloration

It’s possible to have discoloration of your teeth where your braces used to be. This is completely normal because of how tricky cleaning around braces is. This will improve over time with good cleaning habits, especially now that you have your braces removed!

No more food restrictions

Now that your braces are off, you can eat whatever you want! You can bite into an apple without worrying about damaging your braces. Popcorn will no longer get stuck in each and every crevice in your braces. And you can even now chew your favorite bubblegum.

Why Do I Have to Wear Retainers After My Braces Treatment?

Retainers are worn after braces treatment to help keep your teeth in their new position. When you wear braces, the brackets and wires apply pressure to your teeth, slowly moving them into the desired position. However, your teeth are still loose after the removal and can shift back to their original position if neglected. Retainers help to hold your teeth in place and prevent them from moving back to their original position.

Retainers can be removable or fixed. Removable retainers are typically worn for a longer period, often for several months or years, depending on the individual case. Fixed retainers are typically worn for a shorter period, usually a few months. Your orthodontist determines the duration of wearing a retainer. They will monitor your teeth and jaw and make recommendations based on your case.

Retainers are also used to maintain the changes in the jaw and bite. They prevent the teeth from shifting back to the previous position or drifting, which can cause malocclusion and bite problems again.

It’s essential to wear your retainer as instructed to ensure that your teeth don’t drift and that your treatment is successful. With proper care and maintenance, your new smile will last a lifetime.

How to Maintain Your Smile After Orthodontic Treatment

#1 – Brush and floss regularly.

Brushing your teeth twice daily with a soft-bristled toothbrush and using fluoride toothpaste is essential to maintaining good oral health. In addition, it is important to floss regularly as this helps to remove plaque and food particles which can be challenging to reach with brushing alone. These simple measures daily will help you achieve improved oral hygiene and protect against dental decay.

#2 – Wear your retainer as instructed.

Once your braces have been taken off, your orthodontist will likely provide you with a retainer to wear. This device should be worn as directed by your orthodontist to maintain the new position of your teeth and stop them from shifting back into their original place. Wearing the retainer is essential for preserving the results achieved with braces.

#3 – Watch your diet

It is essential to be mindful of what you put in your mouth while wearing braces. Avoiding hard, sticky and sugary foods can help protect your braces from becoming damaged and prevent tooth decay.

Foods like candy, gum, and other sweet treats should be avoided as they can loosen or break off the metal wires on your braces. Sugar-filled snacks can also lead to cavities due to the bacteria that feed off them. Eating a balanced diet of nutritious foods instead will not only keep your teeth healthy and benefit your overall well-being.

#4 – Visit your dentist and orthodontist regularly.

Regular dental check-ups and orthodontic appointments are essential to maintain a good level of oral health. Attending these regular visits will help to ensure that your teeth and gums remain in excellent condition. It also allows your orthodontist in Renton to track the progress of any treatment you may be undergoing.

In addition, keeping up with your appointments is important because it ensures that any issues can be identified early on. This allows for a speedy resolution and prevents further problems from arising.

#5 – Be mindful of your habits.

Habits such as nail-biting, pencil or pen chewing, and teeth grinding can damage your braces. It can also ultimately delay the progress of your orthodontic treatment. Therefore, it is essential to try and break these bad habits as quickly as possible. Doing so avoids further damage that could slow down the time it takes to achieve your desired orthodontic results.

#6 – Maintain good oral hygiene

Good oral hygiene is essential to maintaining your beautiful smile after orthodontic treatment. Brush and floss your teeth regularly, use an antiseptic mouthwash, and consider using dental floss picks and interdental brushes to clean around your braces.

Life After Braces With Lasley Orthodontics

Are you searching for an orthodontist in Renton, WA? Lasley Orthodontics is here to assist you throughout your orthodontic treatment, and that includes life after braces. We’ll ensure that even after your braces treatment, you’ll be given the proper care and instructions to ensure that your results are still in effect. Request an appointment today!

item.title: Braces vs Invisalign in West Seattle: Which Aligns Teeth Better?
item.description: There is a lot of debate surrounding braces and Invisalign in West Seattle, WA. Which method aligns teeth better? Is one more effective than the other? We will explore this topic in-depth and help you decide which orthodontic treatment is best for you! What are braces and Invisalign? Braces are brackets that are bonded to […]
item.content:encoded:
